name: Iron Pickaxe
description: The Iron Pickaxe is a pre-Hardmode pickaxe. It can mine blocks weaker than Meteorite. In Journey Mode, the Iron Pickaxe replaces the Copper Pickaxe for newly created characters. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead Pickaxe. The Iron Pickaxe has slightly less pickaxe power, damage, and mining speed.

name: Dirt Block
description: The Dirt Block is one of the most common blocks in Terraria, occurring naturally almost everywhere throughout the world. All caves above the elevation of 0 ft are made of Dirt. Below 0 ft, in the underground layer, the caves are made of roughly 50% Dirt. Small patches of Dirt can be found in the cavern layer, though it is mostly made of Stone. Dirt Blocks may be covered by different types of grass: regular, Corrupt, Crimson, Hallowed, mowed, or mowed Hallowed. Grass spreads onto adjacent Dirt Blocks, and it can be grown manually using seeds or the Staff of Regrowth or Axe of Regrowth.

name: Stone Block
description: The Stone Block is a type of block that is generated naturally in very large parts of the world. The majority of the Underground and Cavern layers is comprised of Stone Blocks. As a crafting ingredient, they are vital early in the game to craft the Furnace and Wooden Arrows. After Hardmode is activated in a world, nearby Corrupt, Crimson, and Hallowed blocks can spread through Stone Blocks, changing them into Ebonstone, Crimstone, or Pearlstone, respectively. Ebonstone and Crimstone Blocks can be returned to normal with Purification Powder, whereas Pearlstone Blocks require Vile or Vicious Powders. All three types can also be transformed back into regular Stone Blocks with the Green Solution.

name: Iron Broadsword
description: The Iron Broadsword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead Broadsword.

name: Mushroom
description: The Mushroom is a pre-Hardmode crafting material and recovery potion that spawns randomly on grass or Hallowed grass on the surface layer. It can be harvested with any tool or weapon. When used, the Mushroom restores 15 health and inflicts Potion Sickness for 30 seconds. This duration can be reduced via the Philosopher's Stone or Charm of Myths.

name: Iron Shortsword
description: The Iron Shortsword is a pre-Hardmode shortsword. In Journey Mode, the Iron Shortsword replaces the Copper Shortsword for newly created characters. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead Shortsword.

name: Iron Hammer
description: The Iron Hammer is a pre-Hardmode hammer. Newly created characters spawn with an Iron Hammer in Journey Mode. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead Hammer.

name: Torch
description: Torches are furniture items that function as light sources when placed, held, or dropped (via the Throw button). Regular Torches can be found randomly in Chests and Pots, as a bonus drop from slimes, or purchased from the Merchant or Skeleton Merchant for 50 CC each. Placing at least 101 Torches in close proximity below the surface layer in any biome will start the Torch God mini-event. Completing the event will grant the Torch God's Favor, which allows the player to automatically convert ordinary Torches to one of the biome types.

name: Wood
description: Wood is a type of block that is generally obtained by cutting down trees using an axe or a chainsaw. Regular Wood can be harvested from Forest trees, vanity trees, Living Trees, and Underground Cabins in underground or cavern layers. Forest trees can be cultivated by placing Acorns on top of regular grass or Mowed grass above surface, and vanity trees can be cultivated by placing Sakura Sapling or Yellow Willow Sapling on top of any grass (but yields Ebonwood or Shadewood instead if placed on Corrupt or Crimson grass variants). Apart from regular Wood, there are 11 other types of wood. Crafting recipes that include Any Wood as an ingredient can use any type except Dynasty Wood, Feywood, and Pine Wood.

name: Iron Axe
description: The Iron Axe is a pre-Hardmode axe. In Journey Mode(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), the Iron Axe replaces the Copper Axe for newly created characters. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead Axe.

name: Iron Ore
description: Iron Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore. It spawns in the Underground and Cavern layers, and occasionally on the surface. Iron Ore also has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes. It can be obtained from all types of crates. Its alternate ore counterpart is  Lead Ore.

name: Copper Ore
description: Copper Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore. It spawns in the Underground and Cavern layers, and occasionally on the surface. Copper Ore also has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes. Its alternate ore counterpart is  Tin Ore.

name: Gold Ore
description: Gold Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore. It spawns in the Underground and Cavern layers, and occasionally on Floating Islands. Gold Ore also has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes. Its alternate ore counterpart is  Platinum Ore.

name: Silver Ore
description: Silver Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore. It spawns in the Underground and Cavern layers, and occasionally on Floating Islands. Silver Ore also has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes. Its alternate ore counterpart is  Tungsten Ore.

name: Copper Watch
description: The Copper Watch is a low-tier watch. It enables an on-screen display of the in-game time, accurate to the last hour (one minute in real time). Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tin Watch.

name: Silver Watch
description: The Silver Watch is a mid-tier watch. It enables an on-screen display of the in-game time, accurate to the last half hour (half a minute in real time). Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tungsten Watch.

name: Gold Watch
description: The Gold Watch is a high-tier watch. It enables an on-screen display of the in-game time, accurate to the last minute (one second in real time). Its alternate ore counterpart is the Platinum Watch.

name: Depth Meter
description: The Depth Meter is an informational accessory that enables an on-screen display of the player's vertical position, or depth. Depth is relative to the boundary between the Surface and Underground layers, which has a depth of 0 and is displayed as "Level". Depth is shown in the number of feet (1 foot = 1/2 tile) to this boundary, and is measured from the player's feet. The display also shows the name of the current layer. The Depth Meter has a 1/200 (0.5%) chance of being dropped by Cave Bats, Jungle Bats, Ice Bats, Giant Bats, and Spore Bats. Furthermore, it has a 1/80 (1.25%) chance of being dropped from Crawdads(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), Giant Shellies(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), and Salamanders(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions).

name: Gold Bar
description: The Gold Bar is a pre-Hardmode bar. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Platinum Bar.

name: Copper Bar
description: The Copper Bar is a pre-Hardmode bar. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tin Bar.

name: Silver Bar
description: The Silver Bar is a pre-Hardmode bar. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tungsten Bar.

name: Iron Bar
description: The Iron Bar is a pre-Hardmode bar. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead Bar.

name: Gel
description: Gel is a common crafting material dropped by most slimes and Slimed Zombies. Gel is vital due to it being an ingredient in Torches, and is used regularly from the beginning stages all the way through to endgame scenarios. It is also used as ammunition for the Flamethrower and the Elf Melter, and can therefore be stored in the player's ammo slots.

name: Wooden Sword
description: The Wooden Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Wooden Door
description: Doors are furniture items that prevent enemies from entering an area when closed. Players can open them by pressing the ⚷ Open / Activate on them. Doors can be placed in any space so long as it is exactly three blocks high, with ceiling and floor blocks. Friendly NPCs will also open and close doors during the day as they wander about. Some enemies that follow the Fighter AI can open doors, and Goblin Peons can remove them entirely. See Fighter AI § Opening doors and Tall Gates for details.

name: Stone Wall
description: Stone Wall is a background wall made from Stone Blocks. Like other walls, it stops enemies from spawning when it is placed and can be used for houses.

name: Acorn
description: The Acorn is a type of seed used to plant trees. Acorns can be planted on regular green grass, Hallowed grass, Corrupt grass, Crimson grass, Jungle grass, Sand, or Snow, where they will create saplings that will grow into trees, under the right conditions. Acorns are randomly dropped when cutting down or shaking trees and can also be purchased from the Dryad for 10 CC each. Acorns cannot be retrieved after being placed. Destroying the sapling will not yield any items.

name: Lesser Healing Potion
description: The  Lesser Healing Potion is a pre-Hardmode recovery potion that restores 50 health and inflicts Potion Sickness for 60 seconds when used. This duration can be reduced via the Philosopher's Stone or Charm of Myths. It is the first tier of healing potion, preceding the Healing Potion, Greater Healing Potion, Jungle Juice and Super Healing Potion. In addition to being crafted, Lesser Healing Potions can also be found naturally in pots (only in pre-Hardmode), generated chests, Wooden and Pearlwood Crates, or dropped from most early pre-Hardmode bosses. They can also be purchased for 3 SC from the Merchant, or from the Skeleton Merchant during moon phases 2, 4, 6, and 8:    . Naturally-placed Lesser Healing Potions appear frequently on platforms in the Dungeon (along with Lesser Mana Potions). When encountered in placed form, they function as Placed Bottle crafting stations. They also show the "bottle" tooltip on the map. They can be freed with a pickaxe or ⚷ Open / Activate and retrieved, and can be placed by the player by crafting them into Decorative Healing Potions.

name: Life Crystal
description: The Life Crystal is a permanent booster item that permanently increases the player's health capacity by 20, adding a red heart to the player's health bar, while also restoring 20 health. Life Crystals can be found naturally placed in any area in the Underground layer and below, though they are fairly uncommon. They cannot be found in the Underworld or the Dungeon. Life Crystals also have a 1/8 (12.5%) chance to drop from a Golden Crate, or 19/160 (11.88%) chance from a Titanium Crate.

name: Dirt Wall
description: The Dirt Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in most caves and under overhangs on the surface, considered unsafe. It does not drop as an item when destroyed, and can only be obtained via crafting. It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player. The Natural Dirt Wall is an item that places an unsafe Dirt Wall. It is visually identical and is obtained by throwing a Dirt Wall into Shimmer.

name: Bottle
description: A Bottle is a crafting material required to craft most potions. Like the Pink Vase, Mug, and different Cups, placing it (on most "flat-surface items" or a platform) allows it to act as a Placed Bottle where the player can craft potions. Bottles can also be "filled" when standing near water or honey, turning them into Bottled Water or Bottled Honey, respectively. Bottles can be crafted from Glass and found in chests.

name: Wooden Table
description: Tables are a type of furniture which can be placed on the ground, commonly used in conjunction with chairs. They count as a flat surface item, which is one of the required elements in order to make a house. There are 64 craftable tables and 5 noncraftable tables. Some tables can also be found in Underground Cabins, in which they follow the theme of the cabin they are in.

name: Furnace
description: The Furnace is a crafting station used mainly to smelt ores into bars and create various pieces of furniture. It is also a light source. The Furnace smelts all pre-Hardmode ores besides Hellstone, which must be smelted in a Hellforge. The Glass Kiln, Hellforge, and Hardmode Forges are upgraded versions of the Furnace, each with additional crafting recipes. (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) The Furnace can also be purchased from the Merchant when he is in the Jungle biome for 3 SC.

name: Wooden Chair
description: The Chair is a type of furniture item that can be placed on top of a solid block or platform. They count as a comfort item, which is one of the required elements in order to make a house suitable for an NPC. Players can sit in chairs by using ⚷ Open / Activate on them and get a boost on health regeneration, similar to beds and sofas. NPCs can sit on chairs too. As with all furniture that can be sat on, sitting on any Chair will increase the player's Fishing Power by 5.

name: Iron Anvil
description: The Iron Anvil and Lead Anvil are pre-Hardmode crafting stations used mainly to craft weapons and armor. Both anvils can craft the same items. Most of their recipes require metal bars, although they are also used to make other items, such as the Jungle tier of equipment. An anvil is a vital crafting station for most of the game, as it is used to make items from every tier up to Cobalt and Palladium, after which it must be replaced by a Mythril Anvil or Orichalcum Anvil. Both anvils are craftable, but can sometimes also be found in Underground Cabins. The Iron Anvil can furthermore be purchased from the Merchant.

stats.Tooltip: Used to craft items from metal bars

name: Work Bench
description: Work Benches are a type of furniture and crafting station required to craft many essential early-game items. They are also one possible way to fulfill a house's flat surface item requirement. Bottles, Pink Vases, and Mugs can be placed on it and used as a Placed Bottle for crafting potions. The Work Bench is the first crafting station the player can make in the game, and one of the first items overall. There are 60 types of Work Benches which can be crafted, and 5 types which must be picked up from areas such as the Dungeon. Some Work Benches can also be found in Underground Cabins, in which they follow the theme of the cabin they are found in.

name: Goggles
description: Goggles are a piece of armor placed in the helmet slot. Unlike most helmets, Goggles do not cover the player's hair. Due to their low defense, they are often used as vanity items.

name: Lens
description: A Lens is a common crafting material that has a 1/3 (33.33%) chance to drop from Demon Eyes and Wandering Eyes.

name: Wooden Bow
description: The Wooden Bow is a pre-Hardmode bow. Its best modifier is Demonic, as it does not have any knockback and thus cannot get modifiers that affect it.

name: Wooden Arrow
description: The Wooden Arrow is a pre-Hardmode arrow. It has no special properties and is used for crafting a variety of more advanced ammunition. When fired with certain bows, Wooden Arrows will be converted into a special projectile (e.g. the Molten Fury converting them into Flaming Arrows). Some other bows, such as the Pulse Bow, can convert any type of arrow. Wooden Arrows can be crafted, found in Pots and Chests, dropped by Pincushion Zombies, and purchased from the Merchant for 5 CC each. They can also be purchased from the Skeleton Merchant during moon phases 2, 3, 6, and 7.

name: Flaming Arrow
description: The Flaming Arrow is a pre-Hardmode arrow. It has a 1/3 (33.33%) chance to inflict the On Fire! debuff for 3 seconds on enemies.

name: Shuriken
description: The Shuriken is a consumable ranged weapon. It can hit up to 4 targets. Shurikens can be found in Pots and Chests around the world, and can be purchased from the Merchant for 15 CC each.

name: Suspicious Looking Eye
description: The Suspicious Looking Eye is a boss-summoning item that summons the Eye of Cthulhu when used. It can be used in any biome, but only at night. In addition to being crafted, Suspicious Looking Eyes also have a 1/5 (20%) chance of being found in Ivy Chests and Gold Chests found in the Cavern layer, including locked ones found inside the Dungeon.

name: Demon Bow
description: The Demon Bow is a pre-Hardmode bow. It is the Corruption counterpart to The Crimson's Tendon Bow.

name: War Axe of the Night
description: The War Axe of the Night is a pre-Hardmode axe. Its Crimson counterpart is the Blood Lust Cluster.

name: Light's Bane
description: The Light's Bane is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. When swung, it creates a dark slash near the tip of the sword. The slashes deal 50% of the weapon's damage (8 base damage),[1] have 5 armor penetration,[2] pierce once, and will automatically target enemies within a radius of 3.125 tiles.[1] The weapon also has a chance to create a big slash that deals 100% of the weapon's damage. The chance of big slashes occurring is equal to the player's melee critical strike chance.[1] It is the Corruption counterpart to The Crimson's Blood Butcherer. The Light's Bane is faster than the Blood Butcherer, but has a lower size and base damage.

name: Unholy Arrow
description: The Unholy Arrow is a pre-Hardmode arrow. It can pierce 4 enemies, and its damage decreases by 5% per enemy pierced. In addition to being crafted, Unholy Arrows are dropped by the Eye of Cthulhu. Furthermore, they can be purchased from the Arms Dealer at night for 40 CC once the Eater of Worlds or Brain of Cthulhu has been defeated. During Hardmode, they become available during the day as well.

name: Chest
description: Chests are a type of furniture functioning as storage items. They are accessed using ⚷ Open / Activate and can store up to 40 item stacks in rows of  10×4 /   8×5. In addition to being craftable, a number of Chests are placed in random locations during world generation. The loot contained in these Chests is determined during world generation, and will not change if the world enters Hardmode. Chests cannot be broken with items inside (the blocks a filled Chest is placed on would also have this trait). However, the Block Swap feature allows the player to change the type of a Chest without having to destroy it. Some types of Chests can be locked by the player using a Chest Lock. When doing so, they will require a key to open again.

name: Band of Regeneration
description: The Band of Regeneration is an accessory that can be found in Gold Chests in the Underground and Cavern biomes and in Living Mahogany Trees in the Underground Jungle. When equipped, it regenerates 1 health every second. This effect stacks with the player's natural health regeneration, but unlike the natural regeneration, does not slow or stop when the player is moving or being attacked.

name: Magic Mirror
description: The Magic Mirror is a pre-Hardmode tool. When used, it plays an animation before teleporting the player to their spawn point. Compared to the Recall Potion, the Magic Mirror has unlimited uses, but a delay of 0.75 seconds (45 ticks),[1] which is longer than the Recall Potion's 0.167 seconds (10 ticks)[1] before teleporting.[2] The Ice Mirror(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) is a functionally identical alternative to the Magic Mirror.

stats.Tooltip: Gaze in the mirror to return home

name: Jester's Arrow
description: The Jester's Arrow is a pre-Hardmode arrow. It is unaffected by gravity and can pierce an infinite number of enemies, and its damage decreases by 10% per each enemy pierced. In addition to being crafted, Jester's Arrows can be found in Gold Chests in the Cavern layer.

name: Angel Statue
description: Statues are furniture items. While some are purely decorative, others have a functional effect when connected to wires and activated: Depending on the statue, upon activation it may spawn enemies, drop items, or teleport an NPC to the statue's location. Most statues can be found already placed in any area underground (most frequently in the Dungeon, Underground Cabins, and Cavern level), though Angel Statues also appear in Chests. Like all furniture, a pickaxe, a drill, or explosives are used to collect them. There are a total of 117 different statues: 25 decoration statues, 36 text and numerical statues, and 56 functional mechanisms.

name: Cloud in a Bottle
description: The Cloud in a Bottle is an accessory that can be found in Gold Chests in the Underground and Cavern layers. When equipped, it allows the player an extra jump. The extra jump has a 75% jump duration compared to a normal jump, increasing the maximum height the player can reach without other accessories by 5 tiles, for a total of 11 tiles.

stats.Tooltip: Allows the holder to double jump

name: Hermes Boots
description: The Hermes Boots are a pre-Hardmode accessory that can be found in Chests in the Underground and Cavern layers. While equipped, they grant the ability to sprint, allowing the player to reach maximum running speed faster, and they increase the player's maximum movement speed to 31 mph (22.73 tiles/second). The player will leave behind a visual trail of dust clouds as they sprint. The Hermes Boots, Flurry Boots, and Sailfish Boots(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) have the same functionality, and their effects do not stack with each other.

stats.Tooltip: The wearer can run super fast

name: Enchanted Boomerang
description: The Enchanted Boomerang is a pre-Hardmode boomerang. Its best modifier is Godly or Demonic. Both modifiers increase the average damage output by the same amount.

name: Demonite Ore
description: Demonite Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore. It is primarily dropped from the Eater of Worlds, as well as the Eye of Cthulhu in Corruption worlds. Demonite Ore can also be found rarely in small veins underground. When below 0 ft in elevation, a Gold Pickaxe or better is needed to mine it, otherwise any pickaxe can be used. It is the Corruption counterpart to The Crimson's Crimtane Ore.

name: Demonite Bar
description: The Demonite Bar is a pre-Hardmode bar. It is the Corruption counterpart to the Crimson's Crimtane Bar.

name: Heart
description: Hearts (or Candy Apples during Halloween and Candy Canes during Christmas) are common power-ups which can be dropped by slain enemies, broken pots, or as a bonus drop from slimes. Enemies and pots will only drop Hearts when the closest player does not have full health. They are not inventory items, but are instead instantly consumed and restore 20 health. They emit a faint red light. If the player does not have full health but has full mana, the chance of an enemy dropping a Heart is 1/12 (8.33%). If the player also does not have full mana, then this chance becomes 1/24 (4.17%), as Hearts and Stars cannot be dropped at the same time.[1] See Notes for more details. For the Heart drop chance from pots, see Pot § Upon breaking.

name: Corrupt Seeds
description: Corrupt Seeds are seeds used to plant Corrupt grass on Dirt Blocks, which will then spread The Corruption in all directions. They drop from the Eye of Cthulhu and can be purchased from the Dryad for 5 SC each during a Blood Moon in a world with Corruption. In a world with Crimson, Corrupt Seeds can be purchased from the Dryad if she is spoken to in a Hardmode Graveyard. Corrupt Seeds can also be planted on Mud Blocks to create Corrupt Jungle grass.

name: Vile Mushroom
description: The Vile Mushroom is a type of mushroom found on grass in the Corruption, where they can be cut and collected with a single swing from any weapon or tool. Vile Mushroom can only grow when there are proper walls behind the soil block, or no walls at all. Vile Mushrooms cannot be eaten or brewed into any potions; they are used solely to craft Vile Powder. Vile Mushroom plants will be converted into ordinary Mushrooms and Jungle Spores if they are purified using Purification Powder or the Clentaminator.

name: Ebonstone Block
description: Ebonstone is a block type found in The Corruption. Before Hardmode, it is most prominent in the lining of Chasms. It requires at least a Nightmare Pickaxe or explosives to mine (this can be bypassed by using Purification Powder to turn Ebonstone to normal Stone, which is mineable with any pickaxe). Deathweed can grow on it. Ebonstone is the Corruption version of Stone (similar to Crimstone in The Crimson, or Pearlstone in The Hallow). As with Crimstone, Ebonstone can be converted into ordinary Stone via Purification Powder or Green Solution used by the Clentaminator.

name: Grass Seeds
description: Grass Seeds are seeds used to plant regular grass on Dirt Blocks. They are purchased from the Dryad for 20 CC each, while a Blood Moon is not occurring. They can also be obtained from putting Poo into an Extractinator. Grass Seeds can be used by the Rubblemaker to turn into various placeable objects:

name: Sunflower
description: The Sunflower is a large, tall plant which can be found growing naturally on grass, and can be broken with a pickaxe or drill, or by coming into contact with water. It cannot be grown from seeds like most other plants, but is instead placed fully-grown, either naturally upon world generation or by the player. It can also be purchased from the Dryad for 50 SC each, while a Blood Moon is not occurring. Sunflowers can only be placed on regular, Hallowed, Jungle, or Ash grass, at any height. They cannot be placed on Corrupt grass, Crimson grass, Mushroom grass, bare Dirt Blocks, nor in Clay Pots or Planter Boxes.

stats.Buff tooltip: Movement speed increased and monster spawns reduced

name: Vilethorn
description: The Vilethorn is a pre-Hardmode wand. It has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being obtained from destroying Shadow Orbs found in the Corruption or a 1/5 (20%) chance from Corrupt Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) and Defiled Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), which are fished in the Corruption. When used, it extends a 15-tile-long thorn from the player's position, which passes through blocks, pierces infinite enemies, and hits rapidly for up to six times per enemy. The thorn lasts roughly 1.5 seconds, with each individual segment lasting 1 second. Its best modifier is Ruthless. While Mythical gives a wider array of bonuses, damage rounding causes Ruthless to deal more DPS.

stats.Sell: 1 GC50 SC (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) / 20 SC (Old-gen console and 3DS versions)

name: Starfury
description: The Starfury is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/4 (25%) chance of being found inside Skyware Chests on Floating Islands, as well as a 1/4 (25%) chance of being obtained from Sky Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) and Azure Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). When swung, it occasionally summons a star projectile that falls from the sky to the cursor. The star emits light, deals ×1.5 the weapon's damage (37 base damage) but no knockback, and pierces once.[1] The star will pass through all blocks until it reaches the lowest empty space at or above the cursor, after which it will break on the next block it hits. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Causes stars to rain from the sky'Forged with the fury of heaven'

name: Purification Powder
description: Thrown Powders are consumable items that can be used to convert blocks and walls between their pure, Crimson, or Corrupt forms. When thrown, they release a cloud of sparkling powder with a very short range, comparable to that of a broadsword, and produce a fair amount of light. Thrown Powder comes in three variants: Purification Powder is able to revert any Corrupted, Crimson, or Hallowed blocks and walls back into their pure form, such as reverting Ebonstone into Stone, Crimsand into Sand, or Pink Ice into Ice. It is purchased solely from the Dryad for 75 CC each, while a Blood Moon is not active.

name: Vile Powder
description: Thrown Powders are consumable items that can be used to convert blocks and walls between their pure, Crimson, or Corrupt forms. When thrown, they release a cloud of sparkling powder with a very short range, comparable to that of a broadsword, and produce a fair amount of light. Thrown Powder comes in three variants: Purification Powder is able to revert any Corrupted, Crimson, or Hallowed blocks and walls back into their pure form, such as reverting Ebonstone into Stone, Crimsand into Sand, or Pink Ice into Ice. It is purchased solely from the Dryad for 75 CC each, while a Blood Moon is not active.

name: Rotten Chunk
description: The Rotten Chunk is a crafting material that has a 1/3 (33.33%) chance of being dropped from Eaters of Souls, Devourers, Corruptors, and on the Nintendo 3DS version, the Eater of Worlds.

name: Worm Tooth
description: Worm Tooth is a crafting material dropped by Devourers in The Corruption. Unlike most Corruption items, it does not have a Crimson equivalent.

name: Worm Food
description: Worm Food is a boss-summoning item that summons the Eater of Worlds when used. It can only be used in The Corruption or Underground Corruption, at any time.

name: Copper Coin
description: Coins are the main unit of currency in Terraria, primarily used for transactions with NPCs. The currency comprises four different metals, making four types of coins: Copper Coins, Silver Coins, Gold Coins, and Platinum Coins. Coins can also be used as ammunition for the Coin Gun. Coins can be obtained by killing enemies, selling items to or interacting with NPCs, or finding them in multiple forms of loot. Despite coins appearing as the materials copper, silver, gold, or platinum, they are not related to their respective ores and cannot be crafted from them.

name: Silver Coin
description: Coins are the main unit of currency in Terraria, primarily used for transactions with NPCs. The currency comprises four different metals, making four types of coins: Copper Coins, Silver Coins, Gold Coins, and Platinum Coins. Coins can also be used as ammunition for the Coin Gun. Coins can be obtained by killing enemies, selling items to or interacting with NPCs, or finding them in multiple forms of loot. Despite coins appearing as the materials copper, silver, gold, or platinum, they are not related to their respective ores and cannot be crafted from them.

name: Gold Coin
description: Coins are the main unit of currency in Terraria, primarily used for transactions with NPCs. The currency comprises four different metals, making four types of coins: Copper Coins, Silver Coins, Gold Coins, and Platinum Coins. Coins can also be used as ammunition for the Coin Gun. Coins can be obtained by killing enemies, selling items to or interacting with NPCs, or finding them in multiple forms of loot. Despite coins appearing as the materials copper, silver, gold, or platinum, they are not related to their respective ores and cannot be crafted from them.

name: Platinum Coin
description: Coins are the main unit of currency in Terraria, primarily used for transactions with NPCs. The currency comprises four different metals, making four types of coins: Copper Coins, Silver Coins, Gold Coins, and Platinum Coins. Coins can also be used as ammunition for the Coin Gun. Coins can be obtained by killing enemies, selling items to or interacting with NPCs, or finding them in multiple forms of loot. Despite coins appearing as the materials copper, silver, gold, or platinum, they are not related to their respective ores and cannot be crafted from them.

name: Fallen Star
description: The Fallen Star is an item that randomly falls from the sky at night and disappears at dawn (4:30 AM). They can be used to craft Mana Crystals, which permanently increase a player's mana capacity by 20 points each. They are also used in a variety of crafting recipes, and as ammunition for the Star Cannon and Super Star Shooter(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). Hence, Fallen Stars can be stored in ammo slots. Fallen Stars will spawn randomly throughout the entire nighttime, and their spawn rate is determined by two factors: the world size and a multiplier randomly set at nightfall. See Notes for details.

name: Copper Greaves
description: Copper armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Copper Helmet, Copper Chainmail, and Copper Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 48 Copper Bars (144 Copper Ore). The full set grants 6 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tin armor.

name: Iron Greaves
description: Iron armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Iron Helmet, Iron Chainmail, and Iron Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Iron Bars (180 Iron Ore). The full set grants 9 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead armor.

name: Silver Greaves
description: Silver armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Silver Helmet, Silver Chainmail, and Silver Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Silver Bars (240 Silver Ore). The full set grants 13 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tungsten armor.

name: Gold Greaves
description: Gold armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Gold Helmet, Gold Chainmail, and Gold Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 75 Gold Bars (300 Gold Ore). The full set grants 16 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Platinum armor.

name: Copper Chainmail
description: Copper armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Copper Helmet, Copper Chainmail, and Copper Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 48 Copper Bars (144 Copper Ore). The full set grants 6 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tin armor.

name: Iron Chainmail
description: Iron armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Iron Helmet, Iron Chainmail, and Iron Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Iron Bars (180 Iron Ore). The full set grants 9 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead armor.

name: Silver Chainmail
description: Silver armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Silver Helmet, Silver Chainmail, and Silver Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Silver Bars (240 Silver Ore). The full set grants 13 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tungsten armor.

name: Gold Chainmail
description: Gold armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Gold Helmet, Gold Chainmail, and Gold Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 75 Gold Bars (300 Gold Ore). The full set grants 16 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Platinum armor.

name: Grappling Hook
description: Hooks are a class of tools which aid the player in traversing terrain. When used, hooks fire a chain that latches to a surface or platform and pulls the player towards it. A hook is an essential tool which offers significant freedom from terrain height restrictions as well as being a fast movement option, and will often be the first major mobility tool a player acquires. A variety of hooks are available at all stages of the game (see table below). The Grapple hotkey activates the equipped hook in the player's equipment slot, or the first hook from the inventory (from left to right, top to bottom) without requiring it to be in the player's hotbar.Verify

name: Chain
description: Chains are used both for climbing and as components for various crafting recipes. They are used in crafting items such as the Grappling Hook, Sawmill, all types of watches, and chandeliers. Chains can be placed and used like Rope and offer the same ascent and descent speeds, although they cannot be turned into coils like Rope can. Placed Chains are background objects, and much like furniture, do not count as background walls in a house, are not suitable for NPCs, and cannot be actuated. Chains are not destroyed when dropped in lava, despite having a rarity of 00*.

name: Shadow Scale
description: The Shadow Scale is a pre-Hardmode crafting material dropped from the Eater of Worlds (and its Treasure Bag in Expert Mode). It is the Corruption counterpart to The Crimson's Tissue Sample.

name: Piggy Bank
description: The Piggy Bank is a portable storage item which can be purchased from the Merchant for 1 GC. Like other storage items (eg. chests), it contains 40 slots (4 rows of 10) that can be used to store anything. The Piggy Bank requires only a space 2 wide and 1 high for placement, but must be placed on a Work Bench, Table, or platforms (in contrast, the Safe, Defender's Forge(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), and Void Vault(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) can be placed on blocks, but are considerably larger). The Piggy Bank inventory can also be accessed via the Money Trough(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), which summons a temporary floating version of the Piggy Bank, or the Eye Bone(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). Unlike normal Chests, the Piggy bank (along with the Safe, Defender's Forge, and Void Vault) is considered "personal storage". Items contained in a Piggy Bank are attached to the character rather than to a container in the world: The bank can be picked up and moved regardless of the items within. Coins and items stored within it are safe from other players, and will not be dropped on death. They will even travel with a character who goes to a different world. However, if a Hardcore character dies, all items in their personal storage will be deleted with the character and permanently lost.

stats.Tooltip: Can be used to store your itemsStored items can only be accessed by youStored coins can be used in shops

name: Mining Helmet
description: Mining armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Mining Helmet, Mining Shirt, and Mining Pants. The Mining Helmet can be purchased from the Merchant for 4 GC and can also be dropped by Undead Miners with a 1/20 (5%) chance. The Mining Shirt and Mining Pants are dropped from Undead Miners with a 3/25 (12%) chance. The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

name: Copper Helmet
description: Copper armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Copper Helmet, Copper Chainmail, and Copper Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 48 Copper Bars (144 Copper Ore). The full set grants 6 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tin armor.

description: Iron armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Iron Helmet, Iron Chainmail, and Iron Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Iron Bars (180 Iron Ore). The full set grants 9 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead armor.

description: Silver armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Silver Helmet, Silver Chainmail, and Silver Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Silver Bars (240 Silver Ore). The full set grants 13 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tungsten armor.

name: Gold Helmet
description: Gold armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Gold Helmet, Gold Chainmail, and Gold Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 75 Gold Bars (300 Gold Ore). The full set grants 16 defense while equipped. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Platinum armor.

name: Wood Wall
description: Wood Walls are a background wall made from Wood. Like other walls, it stops enemies from spawning when it is placed and can be used for houses.

name: Wood Platform
description: Platforms are special types of blocks that allow movement through the space they occupy. The player normally stands and walks on platforms as if they were ordinary blocks, and they can move down through a platform by pressing the ▼ Down key while standing on one. Players can also walk or jump horizontally through the space occupied by platforms, only treating the platform as "solid" when landing on them from above. Walking enemies can similarly walk on or pass through platforms. Flying enemies like bats will fly up through them, but not down.Verify Certain enemies,Verify including Harpies, ignore them entirely. Wraiths cannot fly up using platforms but are able to hover on top of them. NPCs will always climb up platforms.

stats.Max stack: 9999 (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) / 99 (Old-gen console and 3DS versions)

name: Flintlock Pistol
description: The Flintlock Pistol is a pre-Hardmode gun that can be bought from the Arms Dealer for 5 GC.

name: Musket
description: The Musket is a pre-Hardmode gun. It will always drop from the first Shadow Orb destroyed in a Corruption world (along with 100 Musket Balls) and has a 1/5 (20%) chance of dropping from destroyed Shadow Orbs afterward. It also has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being obtained from Corrupt(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) and Defiled Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) fished in the Corruption. The Musket has a base 12% critical strike chance as opposed to the usual 4%. Its Crimson counterpart is The Undertaker. The Musket deals a much higher damage per shot at a slower fire rate.

stats.Tooltip: 'I have to be ready, said the minuteman'

name: Musket Ball
description: The Musket Ball is a pre-Hardmode bullet. It has no special properties and is used for crafting a variety of more advanced ammunition. Musket Balls can be bought from the Arms Dealer for 7 CC each, or 70 SC for 1000 rounds. They can also be obtained from Shadow Orbs and Crimson Hearts (along with a Musket or The Undertaker), or from a Present (along with a Red Ryder) during Christmas.

name: Minishark
description: The Minishark is a pre-Hardmode gun that can be purchased from the Arms Dealer for 35 GC. When used, it rapidly fires bullets at a slight spread, with each shot having a 1/3 (33.33%) chance of not consuming any ammunition. Its best modifier is Demonic, as it does not have any knockback and thus cannot get modifiers that affect it.

stats.Tooltip: 33% chance to save ammo'Half shark, half gun, completely awesome.'

name: Iron Bow
description: The Iron Bow is a pre-Hardmode bow. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead Bow.

name: Shadow Greaves
description: Shadow armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Shadow Helmet, Shadow Scalemail, and Shadow Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Demonite Bars (180 Demonite Ore) and 45 Shadow Scales. It is the Corruption counterpart to The Crimson's Crimson armor.

name: Shadow Scalemail
description: Shadow armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Shadow Helmet, Shadow Scalemail, and Shadow Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Demonite Bars (180 Demonite Ore) and 45 Shadow Scales. It is the Corruption counterpart to The Crimson's Crimson armor.

name: Shadow Helmet
description: Shadow armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Shadow Helmet, Shadow Scalemail, and Shadow Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Demonite Bars (180 Demonite Ore) and 45 Shadow Scales. It is the Corruption counterpart to The Crimson's Crimson armor.

name: Nightmare Pickaxe
description: The Nightmare Pickaxe is a pre-Hardmode, post-Eater of Worlds pickaxe. It can mine blocks up to Hellstone, Crimstone Blocks, and Ebonstone Blocks. Its Crimson counterpart is the Deathbringer Pickaxe. The Nightmare Pickaxe has slightly less pickaxe power and mining speed.

name: The Breaker
description: The Breaker is a pre-Hardmode hammer. Its Crimson counterpart is the Flesh Grinder.

name: Candle
description: Candles are a type of furniture and light source. Candles can only be placed on flat-surface items. Candles will produce light underwater if placed and can be toggled on and off with ⚷ Open / Activate and by mechanisms. Certain types of candles provide buffs or debuffs. The Water Candle is a special type of candle found in Dungeons which increases enemy spawn rates by 33% and the maximum number of simultaneous on-screen enemies by 50% when placed or held. The Peace Candle reduces spawn rates by 23% in the area and decreases the maximum number of simultaneous on-screen enemies by 30%, in contrast to the Water Candle. The Shadow Candle(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) nullifies the enemy spawn reduction benefits of towns in the area. For Water Candles and Peace Candles, multiple placed candles cannot stack, but the bonuses from holding and placing a candle do.

name: Copper Chandelier
description: Chandeliers are a type of furniture and light source suitable for house lighting requirements that hang from the ceiling. Chandeliers come in many varieties which differ only in appearance and sell value. All chandeliers can be picked up using a pickaxe, drill, or explosives. Chandeliers take up a 3×3 area of tile space and can be turned on and off using mechanisms. The Large Dynasty Lantern is considered a chandelier because of its dimensions.
