name: Mana Crystal
description: The Mana Crystal is a permanent booster item that permanently increases the player's maximum mana by 20, up to a maximum of 200 total. It takes 9 Mana Crystals to reach this maximum. It also restores 20 mana when used, but only as long as the character's maximum mana is below 200. Mana Crystals are created by combining 5 Fallen Stars. It takes 45 Fallen Stars to maximize the player's mana.

name: Lesser Mana Potion
description: The Lesser Mana Potion is a recovery potion that restores 50 mana when used and inflicts the Mana Sickness debuff upon use. It can be purchased from the Merchant for 1 SC each, and can also be found in Wooden and Pearlwood Crates obtained from fishing. Naturally-placed Lesser Mana Potions appear frequently on platforms in the Dungeon (along with Lesser Healing Potions). When encountered in placed form, they function as Placed Bottle crafting stations. They also show the "bottle" tooltip on the map. They can be freed with a pickaxe or ⚷ Open / Activate and retrieved, and can be placed by the player by crafting them into Decorative Mana Potions.

stats.Debuff: Mana Sickness(Desktop, Console, Old-gen console and Mobile versions)

name: Band of Starpower
description: The Band of Starpower is an accessory that increases the player's maximum mana by 20. The Band of Starpower has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being obtained from destroying Shadow Orbs found in the Corruption or a 1/5 (20%) chance from Corrupt Crates and Defiled Crates, which are fished in the Corruption. It can furthermore be crafted.

name: Flower of Fire
description: The Flower of Fire is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron wand that fires a slow, non-piercing fireball that can bounce off blocks up to five times. The fireball dissipates upon contact with an enemy, with a 1/2 (50%) chance to inflict the On Fire! debuff for a duration of 5 seconds. The Flower of Fire has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being obtained from Shadow Chests in The Underworld, and has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being found in Obsidian Lock Boxes obtained from Obsidian and Hellstone Crates. Its best modifier is Mythical.

name: Magic Missile
description: The Magic Missile is a pre-Hardmode wand. When used, it fires a magic missile that follows the cursor when ⚒ Use / Attack key is held. If the ⚒ Use / Attack key is released mid-drag, the missile will be fired in that direction. If the cursor is stationary on release, it is fired directly away from the player. Once it is released, the missile homes in on nearby enemies, creating a small explosion upon contact with an enemy or block. The Magic Missile has a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from the Locked Gold Chests found in the Dungeon, as well as a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from Golden Lock Boxes(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). Its best modifier is Mythical.

name: Dirt Rod
description: The Dirt Rod is a unique tool that is used solely to move Dirt Blocks around, and is unlike the use of ordinary mining tools and block placement. When used while the cursor is over a Dirt Block, the block becomes a Dirt Ball projectile that sticks to the cursor as long as the ⚒ Use / Attack key is held down. The Dirt Ball can be carried through blocks and relocated anywhere on the screen. When released, the Dirt Ball is affected by gravity and also continues to carry the speed it was being moved at previously, allowing it to be "thrown". Upon impact with any blocks, a released Dirt Ball turns back into a solid Dirt Block. The Dirt Rod is purchased from the Dryad for (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 5 GC / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) 20 GC.

stats.Buy: 5 GC (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) / 20 GC (Old-gen console and 3DS versions)
stats.Sell: 1 GC (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) / 4 GC (Old-gen console and 3DS versions)

name: Shadow Orb
description: The Shadow Orb is a light pet-summoning item that spawns a slow-moving Shadow Orb which follows the player around and emits dim, purple light. The orb can move through solid objects and liquids unhindered, but moves very slowly, causing it to fall behind when the player is moving quickly. If it falls far enough behind, it will teleport to the player's location. The Shadow Orb has a 1/5 (20%) chance of dropping from destroyed Shadow Orbs, but it also has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being acquired from Corrupt Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) and Defiled Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), which are obtained by fishing in the Corruption. The Crimson counterpart to the Shadow Orb is the Crimson Heart.

stats.Sell: 1 GC50 SC (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) / 20 SC (Old-gen console and 3DS versions)
stats.Buff tooltip: A magical orb that provides light

name: Meteorite
description: Meteorite is a pre-Hardmode ore typically mined from Meteorite crash sites. A Meteorite crash site is a mini-biome guaranteed to spawn after the Eater of Worlds or Brain of Cthulhu is defeated for the first time.[1] If the boss is defeated between midnight and dawn (12:00 AM – 4:30 AM), then the meteor crashes immediately. Otherwise, it will crash at the next midnight (12:00 AM). Meteorite must be mined using a Tungsten Pickaxe or better (its counterpart, the Silver Pickaxe, does not have enough pickaxe power). Contact with Meteorite blocks causes the player to be inflicted with the Burning debuff, unless the player has the Obsidian Skull accessory (or its upgrades) equipped, or is using an Obsidian Skin Potion.

stats.Tooltip: Burns you when touched without special protection

name: Meteorite Bar
description: The Meteorite Bar is a pre-Hardmode bar. In addition to being crafted, Meteorite Bars can also be found as common items in Shadow Chests, or more rarely in Gold Chests in the Cavern layer that generate close to the Underworld.

name: Hook
description: The Hook is a crafting material used solely to craft the Grappling Hook. It has a 1/25 (4%) chance to drop from Skeletons, Undead Miners, Hoplites, and Undead Vikings in the Marble Caves, Ice biome, and Cavern layer. It also has a 1/30 (3.33%) chance to drop from Piranhas in the Jungle, Underground Jungle, Cavern, and Underground layer.

stats.Tooltip: Sometimes dropped by Skeletons and Piranha

name: Flamarang
description: The Flamarang is a pre-Hardmode boomerang and an upgrade to the Enchanted Boomerang. It has a 1/2 (50%) chance of inflicting the On Fire! debuff to enemies for 3 seconds on hit. Its best modifier is Godly or Demonic. Both modifiers increase the average damage output by the same amount.

name: Molten Fury
description: The Molten Fury is a pre-Hardmode bow. When used, it converts Wooden Arrows into Flaming Arrows, causing them to inflict the On Fire! debuff on hit.

name: Volcano
description: The Volcano is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. When swung, it creates an explosion upon enemy hits, dealing 75% of the weapon's damage (30 base damage) to the enemy and 2 other targets in an area around the enemy.[1] The Volcano has a 1/2 (50%) chance to inflict the On Fire! debuff for 3 seconds, while its explosion is guaranteed to inflict it for the same duration.[2] Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Molten Pickaxe
description: The Molten Pickaxe is a pre-Hardmode pickaxe. It can mine blocks up to Dungeon Bricks, Cobalt Ore, and Palladium Ore. The Molten Pickaxe also emits light and occasionally inflicts the On Fire! debuff on hit enemies. Its best modifier is Light for harvesting purposes and Legendary for combat purposes.

name: Meteor Helmet
description: Meteor armor is a pre-Hardmode mage armor set consisting of the Meteor Helmet, Meteor Suit, and Meteor Leggings. Crafting a full set requires a total of 45 Meteorite Bars (135 Meteorite). The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

name: Meteor Suit
description: Meteor armor is a pre-Hardmode mage armor set consisting of the Meteor Helmet, Meteor Suit, and Meteor Leggings. Crafting a full set requires a total of 45 Meteorite Bars (135 Meteorite). The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

name: Meteor Leggings
description: Meteor armor is a pre-Hardmode mage armor set consisting of the Meteor Helmet, Meteor Suit, and Meteor Leggings. Crafting a full set requires a total of 45 Meteorite Bars (135 Meteorite). The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

name: Bottled Water
description: Bottled Water is a pre-Hardmode crafting material and recovery potion. It can be crafted at a sink or a body of water. Bottled Water mainly serves as the basis of almost every craftable potion in the game, and is also required for crafting flasks. When used, it restores 30 health and inflicts Potion Sickness for 45 seconds. This duration can be reduced via the Philosopher's Stone or Charm of Myths.

name: Space Gun
description: The Space Gun is a pre-Hardmode magic gun. When used, it fires fast green lasers that can hit up to three enemies. The lasers will increase in damage, pierce, knockback and size (only visually) as they travel. While a full set of Meteor armor is equipped, the Space Gun costs no mana to use. Its best modifier is Mythical.

name: Rocket Boots
description: Rocket Boots are a pre-Hardmode accessory that can be purchased from the Goblin Tinkerer for 5 GC. While equipped, they allow the player to hold ↷ Jump in midair to fly for a total of 1.17 seconds.[1] While flying, the boots emit visual flame particles that give off a large amount of light. Flight time is reset immediately when the player lands on any solid object, or latches onto a surface with a hook.

name: Gray Brick
description: The Gray Brick is a pre-Hardmode brick.

name: Gray Brick Wall
description: Gray Brick Wall is a background wall crafted from Gray Bricks. They can only be painted white, black or brown.

name: Red Brick
description: Red Bricks are bricks crafted from Clay Block.

name: Red Brick Wall
description: Red Brick Wall is a background wall crafted from Red Bricks. It stops enemies from spawning where it is placed and, if covering an entire room, can be used to make NPC houses. These can be painted with any available paint color, except Red which will yield no change in the sprite.

name: Clay Block
description: Clay is a block type mainly used as a crafting material. It is found primarily in Surface-level (above 0 elevation) areas, and is more rare at the upper parts of the Underground. Because of this, the largest deposits of Clay can be found simply by exploring the Surface for differently-colored patches in the Dirt. Clay Blocks can be used by the Portable Kiln to turn into various placeable Pots:

name: Blue Brick
description: Dungeon Bricks are a type of brick from which the Dungeon is constructed. On world generation, one of three colors of Dungeon Brick is chosen to create the entirety of the Dungeon. Dungeon Brick is one of very few block types that is impervious to explosions. 100% Pickaxe power (a Molten Pickaxe or better) is required to mine Dungeon Bricks in the Underground-level Dungeon, although any pickaxe can mine Dungeon Bricks at or above the Surface layer, and any Dungeon Bricks placed within the center third of the world. They can also be obtained from Dungeon Crates or Stockade Crates.

name: Blue Brick Wall
description: Dungeon Brick Walls are types of background walls which can be found naturally generated in the Dungeon, considered unsafe. They drop as items when destroyed, and can also be obtained via crafting on  Desktop,  Console, and  Mobile. They are considered safe walls when placed by the player. There are a total of nine different variants (three variants in three colors), but each Dungeon can include only the three wall types matching that Dungeon's color. A single Dungeon will usually contain all three types in different areas; in each area, the faction flags found will correspond to the wall variant.

name: Chain Lantern
description: Lanterns are small light sources that hang from ceilings (the underside of blocks). They occupy less room than a chandelier, making them ideal for smaller spaces. Most lanterns can be toggled on and off via wire. There are two lanterns that provide regeneration buffs, the Heart Lantern and the Star in a Bottle, which grant health and mana regeneration, respectively. Toggling these lanterns off will only deactivate the light source, but not the buff.

name: Green Brick
description: Dungeon Bricks are a type of brick from which the Dungeon is constructed. On world generation, one of three colors of Dungeon Brick is chosen to create the entirety of the Dungeon. Dungeon Brick is one of very few block types that is impervious to explosions. 100% Pickaxe power (a Molten Pickaxe or better) is required to mine Dungeon Bricks in the Underground-level Dungeon, although any pickaxe can mine Dungeon Bricks at or above the Surface layer, and any Dungeon Bricks placed within the center third of the world. They can also be obtained from Dungeon Crates or Stockade Crates.

name: Green Brick Wall
description: Dungeon Brick Walls are types of background walls which can be found naturally generated in the Dungeon, considered unsafe. They drop as items when destroyed, and can also be obtained via crafting on  Desktop,  Console, and  Mobile. They are considered safe walls when placed by the player. There are a total of nine different variants (three variants in three colors), but each Dungeon can include only the three wall types matching that Dungeon's color. A single Dungeon will usually contain all three types in different areas; in each area, the faction flags found will correspond to the wall variant.

name: Pink Brick
description: Dungeon Bricks are a type of brick from which the Dungeon is constructed. On world generation, one of three colors of Dungeon Brick is chosen to create the entirety of the Dungeon. Dungeon Brick is one of very few block types that is impervious to explosions. 100% Pickaxe power (a Molten Pickaxe or better) is required to mine Dungeon Bricks in the Underground-level Dungeon, although any pickaxe can mine Dungeon Bricks at or above the Surface layer, and any Dungeon Bricks placed within the center third of the world. They can also be obtained from Dungeon Crates or Stockade Crates.

name: Pink Brick Wall
description: Dungeon Brick Walls are types of background walls which can be found naturally generated in the Dungeon, considered unsafe. They drop as items when destroyed, and can also be obtained via crafting on  Desktop,  Console, and  Mobile. They are considered safe walls when placed by the player. There are a total of nine different variants (three variants in three colors), but each Dungeon can include only the three wall types matching that Dungeon's color. A single Dungeon will usually contain all three types in different areas; in each area, the faction flags found will correspond to the wall variant.

name: Gold Brick
description: Gold Bricks are a type of brick crafted from Gold Ore. In some worlds, they are found as part of Jungle Shrines in the Underground Jungle. Like the ore they are made of, placed Gold Bricks will sparkle.

name: Gold Brick Wall
description: The Gold Brick Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in Jungle Shrines in some worlds. It drops as an item when destroyed, and can also be obtained via crafting. It is always considered a safe wall.

name: Silver Brick
description: Silver Brick is a type of Brick and an aesthetic building material. Like the ore it is made of, placed Silver Bricks will sparkle. It is not recommended that it be used for building unless the player has silver ore to spare. It can be crafted into Silver Brick Walls.

name: Silver Brick Wall
description: Silver Brick Walls are a type of background wall crafted from Silver Bricks. Enemies will not spawn where they are placed, and they can be used for NPC houses.

name: Copper Brick
description: Copper Brick is a type of Brick and an aesthetic building material. Like the ore used to make it, placed Copper Bricks will sparkle. As copper becomes fairly useless quite early in the game, this is an inexpensive ore brick to build with. Despite being made of Copper, it has no sell value, as with most other bricks. Copper Plating is also available as a decorative block, with the same ingredients and color but a different texture.

name: Copper Brick Wall
description: Copper Brick Walls are a type of background wall crafted from Copper Bricks. Like most walls, they will stop enemy spawning, and can be used for NPC houses.

name: Spike
description: The Spike is a trap that can be found in the Dungeon. Upon contact, it deals 60 damage to the player, on all difficulties, and inflicts the Bleeding debuff for 10 / 20 / 25 seconds. It can be broken with a pickaxe, drill, or explosives. Spikes cannot be destroyed by explosives when the For the Worthy seed is active.[1]

name: Water Candle
description: The Water Candle is a furniture item that can be placed or held to increase enemy and critter spawn rates in the area, and emits a low amount of light. When placed or held, it gives nearby players the Water Candle debuff. Several Water Candles are found commonly on the shelves of the Dungeon. They can also be crafted at a Crystal Ball, which is bought from the Wizard. The Water Candle debuff increases all enemy and critter spawn rates by 33%, and increases the maximum number of simultaneous on-screen enemies by 50%. The debuff is applied to any players holding or standing within a rectangular area of 170×125[1] tiles centered on a Water Candle. In order to be considered "holding" the candle, it must be in the player's hotbar and currently selected; merely being in a player's inventory will not suffice. Multiple candles will not stack with each other, even if one is placed and the other held. Water Candles can only be placed on platforms and flat furniture items like Work Benches and Tables.

stats.Tooltip: Holding this may attract unwanted attention

name: Book
description: The Book is a crafting material used only to craft Bookcases, Pianos, and Librarian Blocks. It can also be placed as decoration on Flat-surface items. Books are found placed on the Dungeon's many shelves, where they can be freed with a pickaxe or drill. They can also be renewably obtained from Dungeon or Stockade Crates, and can be bought from the Wizard for 15 SC each.

stats.Tooltip: Press Up/Down to cycle through placement styles'It contains strange symbols'

name: Cobweb
description: Cobwebs are crafting materials used to craft Silk and other items. Cobwebs can also be placed. Placed Cobwebs catch players who fall into them and reset fall damage. They break after a short time when a player is stuck in them, and they can also be harvested instantly with any weapon or swung tool. Cobwebs are commonly found in underground areas in clusters of 5 or more alongside the edges of walls or ceilings, and are guaranteed to drop a Cobweb item when broken. They are also found in large quantities in Spider Nests, where they regrow over time; however, Cobwebs found there only have a 1/4 (25%) chance to drop when broken.

name: Necro Helmet
description: Necro armor is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron ranged armor set consisting of the Necro Helmet, Necro Breastplate, and Necro Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 135 Cobwebs and 150 Bones. The Ancient Necro Helmet is dropped from Angry Bones and Dark Casters in the Dungeon, at a 1/450 (0.22%) chance. It is functionally identical to and interchangeable with the Necro Helmet. Its appearance is based on the sprite of the Necro Helmet prior to 1.0.3.

name: Necro Breastplate
description: Necro armor is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron ranged armor set consisting of the Necro Helmet, Necro Breastplate, and Necro Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 135 Cobwebs and 150 Bones. The Ancient Necro Helmet is dropped from Angry Bones and Dark Casters in the Dungeon, at a 1/450 (0.22%) chance. It is functionally identical to and interchangeable with the Necro Helmet. Its appearance is based on the sprite of the Necro Helmet prior to 1.0.3.

name: Necro Greaves
description: Necro armor is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron ranged armor set consisting of the Necro Helmet, Necro Breastplate, and Necro Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 135 Cobwebs and 150 Bones. The Ancient Necro Helmet is dropped from Angry Bones and Dark Casters in the Dungeon, at a 1/450 (0.22%) chance. It is functionally identical to and interchangeable with the Necro Helmet. Its appearance is based on the sprite of the Necro Helmet prior to 1.0.3.

name: Bone
description: Bones are crafting materials and consumable ranged weapons. They are dropped by Angry Bones, Dark Casters, Cursed Skulls, and Librarian Skeletons which spawn in the Dungeon. Bones are greatly affected by gravity, and arc similarly to Throwing Knives and Shurikens. Of these three consumable ranged weapons, Bones have the least range (Throwing Knives having the greatest).

name: Muramasa
description: The Muramasa is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron broadsword that has a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from the Locked Gold Chests found in the Dungeon, as well as a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from Golden Lock Boxes(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). When swung, it creates a blue slash upon enemy hits. The slashes deal 50% of the weapon's damage (12 base damage)[1], inflict no knockback, do not pierce, and have 5 armor penetration.[2] Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Cobalt Shield
description: The Cobalt Shield is a pre-Hardmode shield accessory that has a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from Locked Gold Chests found in the Dungeon, as well as a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from Golden Lock Boxes(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). While equipped, it grants 1 defense and immunity to knockback.

name: Aqua Scepter
description: The Aqua Scepter is a pre-Hardmode wand that fires arcing streams of water in quick succession, with each stream being able to hit up to 5 targets. The water streams bounce upwards upon colliding with lava. They pierce infinitely after a bounce, move slower, are less affected by gravity and can last for a significantly longer time.[1] The Aqua Scepter has a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from the Locked Gold Chests found in the Dungeon, as well as a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from Golden Lock Boxes(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions).

stats.Tooltip: Sprays out a shower of water

name: Lucky Horseshoe
description: The Lucky Horseshoe is an accessory that primarily negates fall damage. The Lucky Horseshoe has a 1/4 (25%) chance of being found inside Skyware Chests on Floating Islands, as well as a 1/4 (25%) chance of being obtained from Sky Crates and Azure Crates. The Lucky Horseshoe also grants +0.05 luck.[1] This bonus also applies to, but does not stack with, all of this accessory's upgrades.

stats.Tooltip: Negates fall damage'Said to bring good fortune and keep evil spirits at bay'

name: Shiny Red Balloon
description: The Shiny Red Balloon is an accessory that increases the player's jump duration by 33% and jump speed by 30%, increasing the height that the player can jump from 6 tiles to ~10½ tiles, effectively a ~75% boost. However, it reduces jump height when underwater, from 8 tiles to ~7 tiles. The Shiny Red Balloon has a 1/4 (25%) of being found inside Skyware Chests on Floating Islands, as well as a 1/4 (25%) chance of being obtained from Sky Crates and Azure Crates.

name: Harpoon
description: The Harpoon is a unique pre-Hardmode ranged weapon that auto-fires an arcing spear projectile connected to the player via a chain that pierces through enemies and inflicts the Bleeding debuff for 8–12 seconds on hit. It retracts if it hits a third enemy, collides with solid blocks, has travelled for more than 62.5 tiles away from the player[1], or if the player lets go of the ⚒ Use / Attack button. Its physics are similar to launched flails, though it is highly affected by gravity. The Harpoon has a 1/200 (0.5%) chance of being dropped by most enemies during a Goblin Army. The Harpoon has no cooldown period; its auto-fire rate dependent only on the distance to its impact, meaning that an enemy or object in close range will cause a faster auto-fire rate. The projectile also deals damage and knockback to multiple enemies on its return trip, similar to a flail or boomerang, making it effective for crowd control.

name: Spiky Ball
description: The Spiky Ball is a consumable ranged weapon that is primarily used as a passive trap by tossing them to the ground, where enemies who pass over them will take damage up to 7 times (after which the Spiky Balls vanish). If not destroyed, they will automatically vanish after 1 minute and 20 seconds.[1] They can also be directly thrown at enemies, but they have a very short arc and quickly fall to the ground. Spiky Balls have a 199/400 (49.75%) chance of being dropped by all goblins during the Goblin Army event (except for the Goblin Warlock), and can be purchased from the Goblin Tinkerer for 80 CC each. The Goblin Tinkerer uses Spiky Balls to defend himself against nearby enemies.

name: Ball O' Hurt
description: The Ball O' Hurt is a pre-Hardmode flail that has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being obtained from destroying Shadow Orbs found in The Corruption or a 1/5 (20%) chance from Corrupt Crates and Defiled Crates, which are fished in the Corruption. Its Crimson flail counterpart is The Meatball. Its Crimson Heart melee weapon counterpart is The Rotted Fork.

name: Blue Moon
description: The Blue Moon is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron flail that has a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from the Locked Gold Chests found in the Dungeon, as well as a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from Golden Lock Boxes. If the Sunfury is also in the player's inventory, then the Sunfury will be thrown out at the same time as the Blue Moon, but in the mirrored direction.

name: Handgun
description: The Handgun is a pre-Hardmode gun that has a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from Locked Gold Chests in the Dungeon, and from Golden Lock Boxes found in Dungeon Crates or Stockade Crates.

name: Water Bolt
description: The Water Bolt is a pre-Hardmode spell book that can be found hidden within the shelves of the Dungeon, along with having a 1/40 (2.5%) chance to be dropped by Water Bolt Mimics. When used, it fires a slow-moving water ball projectile. The water ball dissipates after bouncing off blocks up to five times, after piercing nine enemies, or after 30 seconds have passed. Its best modifier is Mythical.

stats.Tooltip: Casts a slow moving bolt of water

name: Bomb
description: The Bomb is an explosive that explodes 3 seconds after being thrown. The explosion breaks nearly all blocks, background walls, and furniture within a 4-tile radius, except explosion-proof objects. The explosion also deals 100 damage to all enemies, critters, and players (but not NPCs). Bombs work in liquids. The Bomb can be purchased from the Demolitionist and Skeleton Merchant for 3 SC each or obtained from Pots, Chests, slimes, by powering Bomb Statues, or as a drop from Undead Miners with a 18/25 (72%) chance. It has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes.

stats.Tooltip: A small explosion that will destroy most tiles

name: Dynamite
description: Dynamite is an explosive that explodes 5 seconds after being thrown. The explosion breaks nearly all blocks, background walls, and furniture within a radius of 7 tiles, except explosion-proof objects. The explosion also deals 250 damage to all enemies, critters, and players (but not NPCs) within 7.8 tiles  horizontally and vertically of the Dynamite.[1] Dynamite works in liquids. Dynamite can be found in Chests or purchased from the Demolitionist for 20 SC each.

stats.Tooltip: A large explosion that will destroy most tiles

name: Grenade
description: The Grenade is an explosive consumable ranged weapon which can be purchased from the Demolitionist for 75 CC each, or found rarely in pots and natural Chests. Once Hardmode is activated, they can be found in Pots (replacing Shurikens). A thrown Grenade will bounce slightly and roll until it either hits a valid target or 3 seconds have elapsed, after which it will explode.

stats.Tooltip: A small explosion that will not destroy tiles

name: Sand Block
description: Sand Blocks are soil blocks found in vast quantities in Desert biomes, at shore and bottom of Ocean biomes, and can be found as microbiomes in patches in the Underground layer. They are also shot as projectiles by Antlions. Sand can spread and be converted by the evil biomes and The Hallow (see below). Sand is subject to gravity, a unique quality only shared by Slush, Silt, Shell Piles, Cracked Dungeon Bricks, and Ash on For the Worthy and Zenith worlds. Sand Blocks will deal damage to any player or enemy they fall on. If the amount of Sand falling on the player is significant, the Suffocation debuff will be inflicted until the player manages to escape. Sand is used to craft a small number of building blocks and other items directly, but it is also the raw material for Glass, which is used for a full furniture set and a variety of other items.

stats.Tooltip: Will suffocate you if it falls on you
stats.Duration: Until the player gets out from under the Sand Blocks

name: Glass
description: Glass is crafted from different types of sand at a Furnace. Glass can be placed as a block and is an ingredient in producing various items.

name: Sign
description: The Sign is a decorative furniture item that can store a player-written message. A Sign must be placed and then the ⚷ Open / Activate button must be pressed on it to edit or display its message. A Sign can be placed on top of blocks, beside them, or hanging from their underside, or be placed on walls. A Sign's message can be up to 10 lines long, at 48 characters max per line, for a total of 480 characters maximum per sign. Signs can be used for decoration, labeling areas, adventure text, etc. There is a maximum limit of 32,000 placed Signs that can be written on in each world.[1] If more than 32,000 are put down, only the first 32,000 Signs that are interacted with can be edited; the rest do not open a menu upon pressing ⚷ Open / Activate (until one of the first 32,000 Signs is removed).

name: Ash Block
description: The Ash Block is a block that covers the upper and lower sections of The Underworld, and can occasionally encase Hellstone. It can be used for building and for growing Fireblossom. (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) In the Ash forests on the outer edges of the Underworld, Ash grass naturally generates on ash blocks, with Ash trees growing on them. Ash Grass Seeds can be planted on ash blocks.

name: Obsidian
description: Obsidian is a pre-Hardmode ore that is created when water and lava collide. Mining Obsidian requires a Gold/Platinum Pickaxe or better. Explosives can also destroy Obsidian. Uniquely, Obsidian has no bar form of its own. It is used to craft Hellstone Bars instead.

name: Hellstone
description: Hellstone is a pre-Hardmode ore that can be found in The Underworld. Mining it requires at least a Nightmare/Deathbringer Pickaxe, and it cannot be mined with explosives in pre-Hardmode. Hellstone releases a half-tile of lava when mined in The Underworld. Hellstone inflicts the Burning debuff on players who touch it, which can be negated through items such as the Obsidian Skull and Obsidian Skin Potion.

stats.Tooltip: Burns you when touched without special protection

name: Hellstone Bar
description: The Hellstone Bar is a pre-Hardmode bar. It is used to craft some of the most powerful equipment available pre-Hardmode, and remains useful, if not necessary, in dealing with the shift in difficulty upon entering Hardmode. Notably, the Molten Pickaxe is the only pre-Hardmode pickaxe capable of mining the first tier of Hardmode ores, Cobalt and Palladium.

name: Mud Block
description: Mud Blocks are a very common block type that comprises the majority of the Jungle, Underground Jungle, and Glowing Mushroom biome. It also generates in irregular patches in the Cavern layer. Mud Blocks may be covered by Jungle grass, Mushroom grass, Corrupt grass, or Crimson grass on any side, which can be planted with Jungle Grass Seeds, Mushroom Grass Seeds, Corrupt Seeds, and Crimson Seeds. Only Jungle grass and Mushroom grass are incapable of growing on Dirt Blocks.

name: Sapphire
description: Sapphire is a gem found within the Underground and Cavern layers of a world. They are obtained by breaking special blocks that have noticeable blue gemstones embedded in it. They can also be extracted from Silt, Slush, or Desert Fossil by putting them in an Extractinator. Rarely, Sapphire can be found in bulk in its own Gemstone Cave mini biome, or in one that contains all of the other gems as well (besides Amber). They can be crafted back into their Stone Block forms at a Heavy Work Bench in a Graveyard biome, and can be obtained by cutting down a Sapphire gem tree. They also have a 2/7 (28.57%) chance to be dropped by Geodes.

name: Ruby
description: Ruby is a gem found within the Underground and Cavern layers of a world. They are obtained by breaking special blocks that have noticeable red gemstones embedded in it. They can also be extracted from Silt, Slush, or Desert Fossil by putting them in an Extractinator. Rarely, Rubies can be found in bulk in its own Gemstone Cave mini biome, or in one that contains all of the other gems as well (besides Amber). They can be crafted back into their Stone Block forms at a Heavy Assembler in a Graveyard biome, and can be obtained by cutting down a Ruby gem tree. They also have a 2/7 (28.57%) chance to be dropped by Geodes.

name: Emerald
description: Emerald is a gem found within the Underground and Cavern layers of a world. They are obtained by breaking special blocks that have noticeable green gemstones embedded in it. They can also be extracted from Silt, Slush, or Desert Fossil by putting them in an Extractinator. Rarely, Emeralds can be found in bulk in its own Gemstone Cave mini biome, or in one that contains all of the other gems as well (besides Amber). They can be crafted back into their Stone Block forms at a Heavy Work Bench in a Graveyard biome, and can be obtained by cutting down a Emerald gem tree. They also have a 2/7 (28.57%) chance to be dropped by Geodes.

name: Topaz
description: Topaz is a gem found within the Underground and Cavern layers of a world. They are obtained by breaking special blocks that have noticeable yellow-orange gemstones embedded in it. They can also be extracted from Silt, Slush, or Desert Fossil by putting them in an Extractinator. Rarely, Topaz can be found in bulk in its own Gemstone Cave mini biome, or in one that contains all of the other gems as well (besides Amber). Topaz is the second-least valuable gem available, being able to craft the Sandgun, a type of ranged weapon, along with the rest of the items that usually all gems have counterparts of. They can be crafted back into their Stone Block forms at a Heavy Work Bench in a Graveyard biome, and can be obtained by cutting down a Topaz gem tree. They also have a 2/7 (28.57%) chance to be dropped by Geodes.

name: Amethyst
description: Amethyst is a gem found within the Underground and Cavern layers of a world. They are obtained by breaking special blocks that have noticeable purple gemstones embedded in it. They can also be extracted from Silt, Slush, or Desert Fossil by putting them in an Extractinator. Rarely, Amethyst can be found in bulk in its own Gemstone Cave mini biome, or in one that contains all of the other gems as well (besides Amber). Out of all available gems, Amethyst is the least valuable gem. They can be crafted back into their Stone Block forms at a Heavy Work Bench in a Graveyard biome, and can be obtained by cutting down a Amethyst gem tree. They also have a 2/7 (28.57%) chance to be dropped by Geodes.

name: Diamond
description: Diamond is a gem found within the Underground and Cavern layers of a world. They are obtained by breaking special blocks that have noticeable light blue gemstones embedded in them. They can also be extracted from Silt, Slush, or Desert Fossil by putting them in an Extractinator. Rarely, Diamonds can be found in its own Gemstone Cave mini biome, or in one that contains all of the other gems as well (besides Amber). They can be crafted back into their Stone Block forms at a Heavy Work Bench in a Graveyard biome, and can be obtained by cutting down a Diamond gem tree. They also have a 2/7 (28.57%) chance to be dropped by Geodes.

name: Glowing Mushroom
description: The Glowing Mushroom is a plant, crafting material, and block that can be found on Mushroom grass in the Glowing Mushroom biome. Glowing Mushroom plants emit blue light. Instead of being placed as a plant, Glowing Mushroom items are placed as solid blocks. Glowing Mushroom items can be obtained by destroying Glowing Mushroom plants, cutting down Giant Glowing Mushrooms, and destroying Mushroom vines and cattails. Doing so will also yield Mushroom Grass Seeds.

name: Star
description: Stars (or Soul Cakes during Halloween and Sugar Plums during Christmas) are common power-ups which can be dropped by any enemy with a chance of 13/24 (54.17%), as long as the player is below maximum mana. They are not inventory items, but are instead instantly consumed and restore 100 mana. Enemies will only drop Stars if the closest player is not at maximum mana. It is also possible to spawn Stars (and their seasonal counterparts) with a Star Statue.

name: Ivy Whip
description: Hooks are a class of tools which aid the player in traversing terrain. When used, hooks fire a chain that latches to a surface or platform and pulls the player towards it. A hook is an essential tool which offers significant freedom from terrain height restrictions as well as being a fast movement option, and will often be the first major mobility tool a player acquires. A variety of hooks are available at all stages of the game (see table below). The Grapple hotkey activates the equipped hook in the player's equipment slot, or the first hook from the inventory (from left to right, top to bottom) without requiring it to be in the player's hotbar.Verify

name: Breathing Reed
description: The Breathing Reed is a tool that aids in underwater breathing, and also functions as a broadsword. It has a 9/40 (22.5%) chance of being found in Water Chests, Ocean Crates, and Seaside Crates. When selected, the Breathing Reed is held above the player. This slows down the rate at which their breath meter depletes, and halves the rate at which they lose health while drowning. The reed's tip does not need to be above the surface of the water for this to take effect. The player's breath meter refills as soon as the tip of the reed is outside of water, but not within a block.

stats.Tooltip: Increases breath time and allows breathing in water

name: Flipper
description: The Flipper is an accessory that allows a player to swim in liquids. When equipped, the player can swim upwards in any liquid by holding or pressing ↷ Jump repeatedly – essentially allowing unlimited multi-jumps while within liquid. It can be found in Water Chests and Ocean/Seaside Crates.

name: Healing Potion
description: The Healing Potion is a pre-Hardmode recovery potion that restores 100 health and inflicts Potion Sickness for 60 seconds when used. This duration can be reduced via the Philosopher's Stone or Charm of Myths. It is the second tier of healing potion, succeeding the Lesser Healing Potion and preceding the Greater Healing Potion, Jungle Juice and Super Healing Potion. In addition to being crafted, Healing Potions can be found naturally in pots during Hardmode and pots in the Underworld in pre-Hardmode. Healing Potions are also dropped by Deerclops, Skeletron, and the Wall of Flesh, and are sold by the Merchant and the Skeleton Merchant in Hardmode for 10 SC.

name: Mana Potion
description: The Mana Potion is a recovery potion that restores 100 mana and inflicts the Mana Sickness debuff upon use. It can be crafted or occasionally obtained from any Crates except for Wooden and Pearlwood Crates. They are also sold by the Merchant in Hardmode for 2 SC50 CC. It is the second tier of mana potion, succeeding the Lesser Mana Potion, and preceding the Greater Mana Potion and Super Mana Potion.

stats.Debuff: Mana Sickness(Desktop, Console, Old-gen console and Mobile versions)

name: Blade of Grass
description: The Blade of Grass is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. When swung, it fires a Leaf Blade projectile that travels upwards in a circle and homes in on enemies. The Leaf Blade projectile can pass through blocks, deals 25% of the weapon's damage (4 base damage), has 20 armor penetration, and can pierce two enemies.[1] The sword and its projectiles both have a 1/4 (25%) chance of inflicting the Poisoned debuff for 7 seconds upon hit.[2] Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Has a chance to poison enemies

name: Thorn Chakram
description: The Thorn Chakram is a pre-Hardmode boomerang. Unlike most boomerangs, it does not necessarily return immediately upon contact with objects, but instead can bounce off blocks before returning. The Thorn Chakram has a 1/2 (50%) chance to inflict the Poisoned debuff. Its best modifier is Godly or Demonic. Both modifiers increase the average damage output by the same amount.

name: Obsidian Brick
description: The Obsidian Brick is a brick-block mainly used for building. It can be crafted at a Furnace or mined by any tier of pickaxe. It is commonly found in the Underworld's Ruined Houses.

name: Obsidian Skull
description: The Obsidian Skull is a pre-Hardmode immunity accessory that grants +1 defense and immunity to the Burning debuff inflicted upon contact with Meteorite, Hellstone, Hellstone Bricks, or Ancient Hellstone Bricks. It does not grant immunity to lava or fire-based attacks. In For the Worthy(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) and Zenith worlds, the Obsidian Skull also protects against the Burning debuff inflicted by contact with Crispy Honey Blocks.

name: Mushroom Grass Seeds
description: Mushroom Grass Seeds are seeds that have a 1/40 (2.5%) chance to drop from harvesting Glowing Mushrooms. The seeds can also be purchased from the Dryad for 1 SC50 CC each if she is spoken to in a Glowing Mushroom biome. Shaking a Giant Glowing Mushroom occasionally yields Mushroom Grass Seeds as well. Mushroom Grass Seeds must be planted on Mud Blocks and can be grown both above and under ground. Once planted, Mushroom grass will begin to spread to adjacent Mud Blocks and grow Glowing Mushrooms, as well as Giant Glowing Mushrooms when enough space is available.

name: Jungle Grass Seeds
description: Jungle Grass Seeds are seeds used to plant Jungle grass in Mud Blocks, which can potentially create an artificial Jungle or Underground Jungle (depending on depth) if enough Jungle grass and plants are created. Jungle Grass Seeds can be found by cutting down plants growing on Jungle grass using virtually any tool or weapon. Jungle Grass Seeds can be used by the Rubblemaker to turn into various placeable objects:

name: Wooden Hammer
description: The Wooden Hammer is a pre-Hardmode hammer. Its best modifier is Light for harvesting purposes and either Agile or Forceful/Strong for combat purposes, due to its unusually low base damage.

name: Star Cannon
description: The Star Cannon is a pre-Hardmode ranged weapon that consumes and fires Fallen Stars. The projectiles are not affected by gravity, pierce an infinite amount of enemies, and dissipate into visual light-emitting particles after 1 minute or upon impact with a block.

name: Blue Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Red Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: White Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Yellow Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Meteor Hamaxe
description: The Meteor Hamaxe is a pre-Hardmode hamaxe. It combines the functionality of a hammer and an axe. Its best modifier is Legendary. However, due to rounding, Legendary, Light, Deadly, Agile, Quick, and Nasty provide equal boosts to mining speed for this tool.

name: Empty Bucket
description: Buckets are tools used to carry water, lava, or honey, allowing them to be removed or placed. With an Empty Bucket, pressing the ⚒ Use / Attack button on any sufficient body of liquid collects it, which replaces the Empty Bucket with the respective Water Bucket, Lava Bucket, or Honey Bucket. Pressing the ⚒ Use / Attack button again will deposit the liquid at the location of the cursor, wherein the filled bucket is again replaced with an Empty Bucket. Each bucket holds one tile of liquid. Shimmer cannot be collected with standard buckets. When an Empty Bucket is dropped as an item into the world (with the Throw button) while it is raining, and there are no background walls behind the dropped item, it will turn into a Water Bucket after some time has passed. Empty Buckets will not fill under Rain Clouds or Magic Droppers.

stats.Tooltip: Contains a small amount of honeyCan be poured out

name: Water Bucket
description: Buckets are tools used to carry water, lava, or honey, allowing them to be removed or placed. With an Empty Bucket, pressing the ⚒ Use / Attack button on any sufficient body of liquid collects it, which replaces the Empty Bucket with the respective Water Bucket, Lava Bucket, or Honey Bucket. Pressing the ⚒ Use / Attack button again will deposit the liquid at the location of the cursor, wherein the filled bucket is again replaced with an Empty Bucket. Each bucket holds one tile of liquid. Shimmer cannot be collected with standard buckets. When an Empty Bucket is dropped as an item into the world (with the Throw button) while it is raining, and there are no background walls behind the dropped item, it will turn into a Water Bucket after some time has passed. Empty Buckets will not fill under Rain Clouds or Magic Droppers.

stats.Tooltip: Contains a small amount of honeyCan be poured out

name: Lava Bucket
description: Buckets are tools used to carry water, lava, or honey, allowing them to be removed or placed. With an Empty Bucket, pressing the ⚒ Use / Attack button on any sufficient body of liquid collects it, which replaces the Empty Bucket with the respective Water Bucket, Lava Bucket, or Honey Bucket. Pressing the ⚒ Use / Attack button again will deposit the liquid at the location of the cursor, wherein the filled bucket is again replaced with an Empty Bucket. Each bucket holds one tile of liquid. Shimmer cannot be collected with standard buckets. When an Empty Bucket is dropped as an item into the world (with the Throw button) while it is raining, and there are no background walls behind the dropped item, it will turn into a Water Bucket after some time has passed. Empty Buckets will not fill under Rain Clouds or Magic Droppers.

stats.Tooltip: Contains a small amount of honeyCan be poured out

name: Jungle Rose
description: The Jungle Rose is a vanity accessory which can be harvested from the floor of the Jungle and Underground Jungle. Certain small red flowers growing on Jungle grass have a 1/20 (5%) chance to drop the item when cut.[1] The Jungle Rose cannot be reforged, and therefore cannot have modifiers.

name: Stinger
description: The Stinger is a crafting material used in various Jungle-themed crafting recipes. It is obtained from Spiked Jungle Slimes, Hornets, and Moss Hornets found in the Underground Jungle biome. Excluding the consumable items, crafting all items requires 58 Stingers.

name: Vine
description: Vines are crafting materials dropped by Man Eaters in the Underground Jungle biome with a 1/2 (50%) / 100% drop chance.

name: Feral Claws
description: Feral Claws are a pre-Hardmode accessory that can be found in Jungle Crates, Bramble Crates, and Ivy Chests located in Jungle Shrines and at the base of a Living Mahogany Tree in the Underground Jungle. While equipped, it grants the following effects:

stats.Tooltip: 12% increased melee speedEnables auto swing for melee weapons

name: Anklet of the Wind
description: The Anklet of the Wind is an accessory that gives a 10% bonus to movement speed. The effect also stacks with the movement speed bonus of the Aglet. It can be found in Jungle and Bramble Crates with a 19% chance, and also in Ivy Chests located in Jungle Shrines and at the base of a Living Mahogany Tree in the Underground Jungle with a 21.72% chance.

name: Staff of Regrowth
description: The Staff of Regrowth is a tool that can be found in Ivy Chests as well as Jungle and Bramble Crates. When harvesting blooming stage herbs, it increases the yield of herbs and their seeds. The Staff of Regrowth can also be used on Dirt Blocks to plant grass without the need for Grass Seeds. Unlike other tools, it will not break vegetation when swung, unless directly targeting the tile. The Staff of Regrowth will automatically replant harvested herbs placed in a Clay Pot or a Planter Box. Its best modifier is Light for harvesting purposes and Legendary for combat purposes.

stats.Tooltip: Creates grass on dirtIncreases alchemy plant collection when used to gather

name: Hellstone Brick
description: Hellstone Bricks are placeable blocks that occur naturally in unique Ruined Houses, though they can also be crafted. Like plain Hellstone, players without an Obsidian Skull or one of its upgrades in their accessory slot will receive the Burning debuff from making contact with the brick. Mining the block or carrying it in one's inventory incurs no negative effect, however. As with most damaging blocks, NPCs and enemies are immune to this damage.

stats.Tooltip: Burns you when touched without special protection

name: Whoopie Cushion
description: The Whoopie Cushion is a novelty item and vanity accessory that makes a farting sound when used or when the player takes damage while it is equipped. It has a 1/50 (2%) / 1/25 (4%) chance of being dropped by Giant Worms and Diggers in the Underground and Cavern layers. It can also be obtained by throwing a Zombie Arm into Shimmer.

name: Shackle
description: The Shackle is an accessory that grants 1 point of defense. It is dropped by most kinds of Zombies with a 1/50 (2%) chance. Shackles may be useful early in the game when only few accessories are available. Typically, they should be replaced with more valuable accessories once out of free slots.

name: Molten Hamaxe
description: The Molten Hamaxe is a pre-Hardmode hamaxe. It has a chance to inflict the On Fire! debuff on enemy hits. Its best modifier is Legendary. However, due to rounding, Legendary, Light, Deadly, Agile, Quick, and Nasty provide equal boosts to mining speed for this tool.

name: Flamelash
description: The Flamelash is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron wand. When used, it fires a fireball that follows the cursor when ⚒ Use / Attack key is held. If the ⚒ Use / Attack key is released mid-drag, the fireball will be fired in that direction. If the cursor is stationary on release, it is fired directly away from the player. Once it is released, the fireball homes in on nearby enemies and can pierce once, creating a small explosion upon contact with a block or second enemy. It also has a 1/2 (50%) chance of inflicting the On Fire! debuff for 4–8 seconds. The Flamelash has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being found in Shadow Chests in the Underworld, as well as a 1/5 (20%) chance of being found in Obsidian Lock Boxes(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) obtained from Obsidian Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) and Hellstone Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). Its best modifier is Mythical.

stats.Tooltip: Summons a controllable ball of fire

name: Phoenix Blaster
description: The Phoenix Blaster is a pre-Hardmode gun and the direct upgrade to the Handgun, with increased damage and velocity but slightly less knockback.

name: Sunfury
description: The Sunfury is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron flail that has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being found in Shadow Chests in the Underworld, as well as a 1/5 (20%) chance of being found in Obsidian Lock Boxes obtained from Obsidian Crates and Hellstone Crates. It has a 1/4 (25%) chance to inflict the On Fire! debuff for 3 seconds on hit. The Sunfury also has a base 11% critical strike chance, as opposed to the usual 4%. If the Blue Moon is also in the player's inventory, then the Blue Moon will be thrown out at the same time as the Sunfury, but in the mirrored direction.

name: Hellforge
description: The Hellforge is a crafting station that functions as a regular Furnace, with the additional ability of smelting Hellstone into Hellstone Bars. It is also used to craft the essential Adamantite/Titanium Forge in Hardmode. The Hellforge cannot be crafted, and is instead found in Ruined Houses in the Underworld or obtained from Obsidian Crates or Hellstone Crates. It can only be collected from the Underworld with tools with at least 65% pickaxe power, i.e. a Deathbringer/Nightmare Pickaxe or better is required. It cannot be broken by explosives during pre-Hardmode.

name: Clay Pot
description: The Clay Pot is a furniture item in which a herb or flower seed can be planted. If left empty, wild grass plants will randomly grow out of it, provided the pot is placed on the surface or in Space.[1] The plant in a Clay Pot cannot be cut in the regular manner, e.g. via nearby swinging tools. Instead, it needs to be removed with a pickaxe, a drill, the Staff of Regrowth, or the Axe of Regrowth(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions).

name: Nature's Gift
description: Nature's Gift is an accessory that reduces the mana cost of using magic weapons and other mana-consuming items by 6%. It is acquired by harvesting a rare flower that grows on Jungle grass in the Underground Jungle, using any weapon or tool. The flower appears as a blue version of the Jungle Rose. Nature's Gifts can be used by the Rubblemaker to put themselves back in a placed state:

name: Bed
description: Beds are furniture items which allow the player to set a new spawn point by pressing the ⚷ Open / Activate button on the foot half of the Bed. This requires it to be placed in a mostly valid house (see details below). On the  Desktop version,  Console version, and  Mobile version, Beds furthermore allow the player to sleep in them by pressing the ⚷ Open / Activate button on the head half of the Bed. This makes time pass five times faster and gives a boost to health regeneration, similar to chairs and Sofas. Beds fulfill the chair (comfort item) requirement for the purposes of NPC housing. Like most other furniture, the direction a Bed is placed in depends on the direction the player is facing when placing it.

name: Silk
description: Silk is an item used to craft the Bed, as well as several placeable decorative items and vanity clothing items.

name: Lesser Restoration Potion
description: The Lesser Restoration Potion is a recovery potion that combines the effects of the Lesser Healing Potion and Lesser Mana Potion. On the  Desktop version,  Console version, and  Mobile version, Lesser Restoration Potions were removed after updating to any version equal to 1.4.0.1. Any Lesser Restoration Potions existing in world are replaced with Restoration Potions.

name: Restoration Potion
description: The Restoration Potion is a recovery potion that can be found in Shadow Chests in the Underworld. When used, it restores 90 health and inflicts Potion Sickness for only 45 seconds (rather than the usual 60 of health-restoring potions). The duration of Potion Sickness can be reduced to 33.75 seconds via the Philosopher's Stone or Charm of Myths. Despite its appearance, the Restoration Potion is not created by upgrading a Lesser Restoration Potion.

name: Jungle Hat
description: Jungle armor is a pre-Hardmode mage armor set consisting of the Jungle Shirt, Jungle Hat, and Jungle Pants. Crafting a full set requires a total of 32 Jungle Spores, 2 Vines, and 10 Stingers. Jungle armor pieces are interchangeable with pieces from the Ancient Cobalt armor set, still providing the set bonus but not the grass particle effect.

stats.Tooltip: Increases maximum mana by 206% increased magic critical strike chance

name: Jungle Shirt
description: Jungle armor is a pre-Hardmode mage armor set consisting of the Jungle Shirt, Jungle Hat, and Jungle Pants. Crafting a full set requires a total of 32 Jungle Spores, 2 Vines, and 10 Stingers. Jungle armor pieces are interchangeable with pieces from the Ancient Cobalt armor set, still providing the set bonus but not the grass particle effect.

stats.Tooltip: Increases maximum mana by 206% increased magic critical strike chance

name: Jungle Pants
description: Jungle armor is a pre-Hardmode mage armor set consisting of the Jungle Shirt, Jungle Hat, and Jungle Pants. Crafting a full set requires a total of 32 Jungle Spores, 2 Vines, and 10 Stingers. Jungle armor pieces are interchangeable with pieces from the Ancient Cobalt armor set, still providing the set bonus but not the grass particle effect.

stats.Tooltip: Increases maximum mana by 206% increased magic critical strike chance

name: Molten Helmet
description: Molten armor is a pre-Hardmode melee armor set consisting of the Molten Helmet, Molten Breastplate, and Molten Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 45 Hellstone Bars (135 Hellstone and 45 Obsidian). The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

stats.Set Bonus: 10% extra melee damageGrants immunity to fire blocksCannot be set on fire

name: Molten Breastplate
description: Molten armor is a pre-Hardmode melee armor set consisting of the Molten Helmet, Molten Breastplate, and Molten Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 45 Hellstone Bars (135 Hellstone and 45 Obsidian). The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

stats.Set Bonus: 10% extra melee damageGrants immunity to fire blocksCannot be set on fire

name: Molten Greaves
description: Molten armor is a pre-Hardmode melee armor set consisting of the Molten Helmet, Molten Breastplate, and Molten Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 45 Hellstone Bars (135 Hellstone and 45 Obsidian). The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

stats.Set Bonus: 10% extra melee damageGrants immunity to fire blocksCannot be set on fire

name: Meteor Shot
description: The Meteor Shot is a pre-Hardmode bullet. When fired, it can either bounce from a solid block once or pierce through one enemy.

name: Sticky Bomb
description: The Sticky Bomb is an explosive that functions like the Bomb, but sticks onto any block on contact. If the block it sticks to is removed by some means, it will fall until it hits another.

stats.Tooltip: A small explosion that will destroy most tiles'Tossing may be difficult.'

name: Black Lens
description: The Black Lens is a crafting material dropped by Demon Eyes and Wandering Eyes at a 1/100 (1%) chance.

name: Sunglasses
description: The Sunglasses are a vanity item equipped in the social helmet slot. While equipped, the sun in the background will change to its pre-1.2 variant wearing sunglasses as well. Cattiva will also appear wearing sunglasses when equipped.

name: Wizard Hat
description: The Wizard Hat is a pre-Hardmode armor piece that can be equipped in the helmet slot. While equipped, it grants +4 defense and increases the player's magic damage by 5%. It is always dropped by Tim. The Wizard Hat is part of the Wizard set, which further consists of a Mystic Robe or any gem-augmented Robe. The Wizard set provides a set bonus of +10% magic critical strike chance with the Wizard Hat equipped. The Wizard Hat's alternative is the Magic Hat.

name: Top Hat
description: The Tuxedo set is a vanity set consisting of the Top Hat, Tuxedo Shirt and Tuxedo Pants. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance. While the other two pieces are crafted, the Top Hat is instead dropped by The Groom.

name: Tuxedo Shirt
description: The Tuxedo set is a vanity set consisting of the Top Hat, Tuxedo Shirt and Tuxedo Pants. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance. While the other two pieces are crafted, the Top Hat is instead dropped by The Groom.

name: Tuxedo Pants
description: The Tuxedo set is a vanity set consisting of the Top Hat, Tuxedo Shirt and Tuxedo Pants. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance. While the other two pieces are crafted, the Top Hat is instead dropped by The Groom.

name: Summer Hat
description: The Summer Hat is a vanity item equipped in the Social Helmet Slot. It is purchased from the Clothier for 1 GC, when he is spoken to during the day outside of a Graveyard.

name: Bunny Hood
description: The Bunny Hood is a vanity headgear item. It has a 1/75 (1.33%) chance of being dropped by Corrupt Bunnies and Vicious Bunnies(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). On the Nintendo 3DS version, it can also be dropped by Diseaster Bunnies or Lepus.

name: Plumber's Hat
description: The Plumber's set is a vanity set made up of the Plumber's Hat, the Plumber's Shirt, and the Plumber's Pants. The hat has a 1/250 (0.4%) chance to be dropped by Fire Imps, while the shirt and pants are purchased from the Clothier for 25 GC each, during a full moon. These items are purely vanity items and provide no increase in defense or other bonuses.

name: Plumber's Shirt
description: The Plumber's set is a vanity set made up of the Plumber's Hat, the Plumber's Shirt, and the Plumber's Pants. The hat has a 1/250 (0.4%) chance to be dropped by Fire Imps, while the shirt and pants are purchased from the Clothier for 25 GC each, during a full moon. These items are purely vanity items and provide no increase in defense or other bonuses.

name: Plumber's Pants
description: The Plumber's set is a vanity set made up of the Plumber's Hat, the Plumber's Shirt, and the Plumber's Pants. The hat has a 1/250 (0.4%) chance to be dropped by Fire Imps, while the shirt and pants are purchased from the Clothier for 25 GC each, during a full moon. These items are purely vanity items and provide no increase in defense or other bonuses.

name: Hero's Hat
description: The Hero's set is a vanity set composed of the Hero's Hat, the Hero's Shirt, and the Hero's Pants. Crafting the entire set requires 420 Cobwebs and 27 Jungle Grass Seeds as base ingredients. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance.

name: Hero's Shirt
description: The Hero's set is a vanity set composed of the Hero's Hat, the Hero's Shirt, and the Hero's Pants. Crafting the entire set requires 420 Cobwebs and 27 Jungle Grass Seeds as base ingredients. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance.

name: Hero's Pants
description: The Hero's set is a vanity set composed of the Hero's Hat, the Hero's Shirt, and the Hero's Pants. Crafting the entire set requires 420 Cobwebs and 27 Jungle Grass Seeds as base ingredients. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance.

name: Fish Bowl
description: The Fish Bowl, Gold Fish Bowl (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), Pupfish Bowl (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), and Lava Serpent Bowl (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) are decorative furniture items. The Fish Bowl and Gold Fish Bowl also double as wearable items that can be equipped in the player's helmet slot. Fish Bowls cause only visual effects when placed in the helmet vanity slot. When instead placed in the functional head armor slot, the breath meter appears as if the player were underwater, and if worn long enough the player will drown and die. Breathing aids like the Breathing Reed or Gills Potion will allow for normal breathing when wearing the bowl.

stats.Tooltip: 'No, you can't wear it on your head'

name: Archaeologist's Hat
description: The Archaeologist's set is a vanity set composed of the Archaeologist's Hat, the Archaeologist's Jacket and the Archaeologist's Pants. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance. While two of the pieces are crafted, the Archaeologist's Hat is not, but rather has a guaranteed chance to drop from Doctor Bones.

name: Archaeologist's Jacket
description: The Archaeologist's set is a vanity set composed of the Archaeologist's Hat, the Archaeologist's Jacket and the Archaeologist's Pants. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance. While two of the pieces are crafted, the Archaeologist's Hat is not, but rather has a guaranteed chance to drop from Doctor Bones.

name: Archaeologist's Pants
description: The Archaeologist's set is a vanity set composed of the Archaeologist's Hat, the Archaeologist's Jacket and the Archaeologist's Pants. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance. While two of the pieces are crafted, the Archaeologist's Hat is not, but rather has a guaranteed chance to drop from Doctor Bones.

name: Black Thread
description: Threads are crafting materials used primarily in vanity items. Black Thread and Pink Thread can be purchased from the Clothier, while Green Thread can be crafted.

name: Green Thread
description: Threads are crafting materials used primarily in vanity items. Black Thread and Pink Thread can be purchased from the Clothier, while Green Thread can be crafted.

name: Ninja Hood
description: Ninja armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Ninja Hood, Ninja Shirt and Ninja Pants. King Slime drops each piece separately (1 per kill with a 1/3 (33.33%) chance each), or two pieces from the Treasure Bag it drops in Expert Mode. The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

name: Ninja Shirt
description: Ninja armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Ninja Hood, Ninja Shirt and Ninja Pants. King Slime drops each piece separately (1 per kill with a 1/3 (33.33%) chance each), or two pieces from the Treasure Bag it drops in Expert Mode. The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

name: Ninja Pants
description: Ninja armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Ninja Hood, Ninja Shirt and Ninja Pants. King Slime drops each piece separately (1 per kill with a 1/3 (33.33%) chance each), or two pieces from the Treasure Bag it drops in Expert Mode. The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

name: Leather
description: Leather is a crafting material that is used to create the Archaeologist's Jacket and the Archaeologist's Pants. It is made using Rotten Chunks, a material only obtainable in Corruption worlds. It cannot be obtained in a Crimson world unless the player makes an artificial Corruption biome (e.g. using Corrupt Seeds, purchased from the Dryad in a Graveyard(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions)) or uses a Corrupt Statue.

name: Red Hat
description: The Clothier's set is a vanity set consisting of the Red Hat, Clothier's Jacket(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), and Clothier's Pants(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance. The Red Hat will always drop from the Clothier NPC. The other parts of the set are sold by the Clothier for 3 GC each, during Halloween.

name: Goldfish
description: The Goldfish is a critter that swims in water and walks on land during rain. It flops in place when out of water and rain; it does not take damage while doing so. The Goldfish has only 5 health and no defense, and can be damaged by other enemies and players. The Goldfish can be caught with any Bug Net to be carried around in the inventory and released later, or crafted into animated decorative furniture (Fish Bowl). Once crafted, the Goldfish cannot be released again, unless the furniture item is de-crafted via Shimmer(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions).

stats.Tooltip: 'It's smiling, might be a good snack'
stats.Sell: 7 SC50 CC (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions)1 SC (Old-gen console version)No Value (3DS version)

name: Robe
description: The Robe is a vanity item equipped in the shirt slot. It can be upgraded with gems to a piece of the Wizard set. It resembles the robes worn by Dark Casters and Tim.

name: Robot Hat
description: The Robot Hat is a vanity item equipped in the Social Helmet Slot. It has a 1/250 (0.4%) chance to drop from Piranhas and Angler Fish.

name: Gold Crown
description: The Gold Crown is a craftable vanity item that can be worn in the Social Helmet inventory slot. It is also one of the crafting materials for the Slime Crown, which is used to spawn King Slime. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Platinum Crown.

name: Hellfire Arrow
description: The Hellfire Arrow is a pre-Hardmode arrow that explodes upon impact, damaging all enemies in a small area. Hellfire Arrows can be found in Shadow Chests and Pots in The Underworld, crafted at an anvil, or purchased from the Demolitionist for 1 SC each in Hardmode.

name: Sandgun
description: The Sandgun is a pre-Hardmode ranged weapon that fires sand ball projectiles that are affected by gravity. Upon contact with an enemy or block, the sand ball turns into its respective form of block (if the space is not occupied).[1] Its sand balls have varying effects depending on which sand block is used for ammunition:

name: Guide Voodoo Doll
description: The Guide Voodoo Doll is a boss-summoning item used to summon the Wall of Flesh. It is a guaranteed drop from Voodoo Demons, a fairly rare enemy in the Underworld. If the item is dropped and destroyed by lava in the Underworld, the Guide will instantly die and the Wall of Flesh will be summoned. The Guide must be alive for this process to occur. If the item is destroyed by lava elsewhere, the Guide will die as normal but the boss will not be summoned; if the item is destroyed by lava when the Guide is not alive, the item will be wasted without summoning the boss.

name: Diving Helmet
description: The Diving Helmet is an armor piece for the helmet slot. It has a 1/20 (5%) chance of being dropped by Sharks and Orcas. When equipped, it causes the breath meter to deplete much slower and slows the rate at which drowning damage is taken.

name: Familiar Shirt
description: The Familiar set is a vanity set that displays the player's default clothes. It consists of the Familiar Wig, the Familiar Shirt, and the Familiar Pants, and can be purchased from the Clothier for 3 GC (set). When a piece of the Familiar set is equipped in the vanity armor slot, it will disable the graphics of any item worn in the corresponding armor slot. As such, the Familiar Wig will show the character's hairstyle whereas the Familiar Shirt and Familiar Pants will show the character's default shirt and pants, respectively.

name: Familiar Pants
description: The Familiar set is a vanity set that displays the player's default clothes. It consists of the Familiar Wig, the Familiar Shirt, and the Familiar Pants, and can be purchased from the Clothier for 3 GC (set). When a piece of the Familiar set is equipped in the vanity armor slot, it will disable the graphics of any item worn in the corresponding armor slot. As such, the Familiar Wig will show the character's hairstyle whereas the Familiar Shirt and Familiar Pants will show the character's default shirt and pants, respectively.

name: Familiar Wig
description: The Familiar set is a vanity set that displays the player's default clothes. It consists of the Familiar Wig, the Familiar Shirt, and the Familiar Pants, and can be purchased from the Clothier for 3 GC (set). When a piece of the Familiar set is equipped in the vanity armor slot, it will disable the graphics of any item worn in the corresponding armor slot. As such, the Familiar Wig will show the character's hairstyle whereas the Familiar Shirt and Familiar Pants will show the character's default shirt and pants, respectively.

name: Demon Scythe
description: The Demon Scythe is a pre-Hardmode spell book that has a 1/35 (2.86%) chance of being dropped by Demons and Voodoo Demons. When used, it fires a sickle projectile that is initially static before gradually accelerating. The sickles can hit up to five times before dissipating, and they cannot pass through blocks. Its best modifier is Mythical.

name: Night's Edge
description: The Night's Edge is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron broadsword. When swung, a circular purple aura follows the swing, reaching slightly farther than the sword itself. Another aura swings around in front, further increasing range. The auras can hit up to two enemies each, but the same enemy cannot be hit by both auras. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Dark Lance
description: The Dark Lance is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron spear that has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being found in Shadow Chests in the Underworld, as well as a 1/5 (20%) chance of being found in Obsidian Lock Boxes obtained from Obsidian Crates and Hellstone Crates. The Dark Lance inflicts the Shadowflame debuff for 4–5 seconds and creates a small shockwave at its tip. Its best modifier is Godly or Demonic. Both modifiers increase the average damage output by the same amount.

name: Coral
description: Corals are crafting materials which can be found in the Ocean biomes at the ends of the world. They grow naturally on Sand Blocks underwater, within 380 tiles of the left or right edges of the world, and above the depth of (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) −20 feet / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) 0 feet, as long as no more than 5 Corals ((Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) or Seashells) are within 7 tiles horizontally and vertically. Corals can be harvested either with a pickaxe/drill/explosives, by mining out the sand underneath them, or by placing a block above them. Afterwards, they can be placed on any type of block and will take on a random appearance out of six possible varieties.

stats.Tooltip: Press Up/Down to cycle through placement styles

name: Cactus
description: Cactus is a crafting material obtained by harvesting Cactus plants, using an axe or chainsaw just like with trees. In addition to crafting, Cactus can be used as a building material. Cacti can be used by the Rubblemaker to turn into various placeable objects:

name: Trident
description: The Trident is a pre-Hardmode spear that has a 9/40 (22.5%) chance of being found in Water Chests, Ocean Crates, and Seaside Crates. When held while submerged in water or lava, the player is granted increased movement speed and jump height. The player can decrease fall speed by holding ▲ Up. These effects do not work in honey or Shimmer.[1] Its best modifier is Godly or Demonic. Both modifiers increase the average damage output by the same amount.

stats.Tooltip: Increases mobility in water when heldHold Up to descend slower

name: Silver Bullet
description: The Silver Bullet is a pre-Hardmode bullet. It deals 2 more damage and is slightly faster than Musket Balls. It also deals 300% damage to Werewolves.[1] Silver Bullets can be bought from the Arms Dealer for 15 CC each, or 1 GC50 SC for 1000 rounds. During pre-Hardmode, they are only sold during Blood Moons (and only in worlds with Silver). Once Hardmode begins, they are always available. Silver Bullets can also be found replacing Wooden Arrows in Pots during Hardmode, in Shadow Chests, or crafted.

name: Throwing Knife
description: The Throwing Knife is a consumable ranged weapon. It can hit up to 2 targets. It can be purchased from the Merchant during a Blood Moon for 50 CC each and may also be found in Chests. The Throwing Knife is greatly affected by gravity and arcs similarly to Shurikens and Bones. It has the greatest range out of these consumable ranged weapons (with Bones having the least). The Merchant will use Throwing Knives to defend himself against nearby enemies.

name: Spear
