description: The Spear is a pre-Hardmode spear that has a 1/10 (10%) chance of being found in Wooden Chests that are located on the surface as well as in the underground layer. Its best modifier is Godly or Demonic. Both modifiers increase the average damage output by the same amount.

name: Blowpipe
description: The Blowpipe is a pre-Hardmode ranged weapon that can found randomly in Wooden Chests near the Surface or in the Underground layer. When used, it fires Seeds and darts as ammunition. While a Blowpipe is in a player's inventory, there is a chance that any vegetation growing on normal grass, in Clay Pots, and in Planter Boxes will drop Seeds. Flowers typically drop 4 Seeds at a time, while tall grass typically drops 1–2 Seeds.

stats.Tooltip: Allows the collection of seeds for ammo

name: Glowstick
description: The Glowstick is a consumable light source that can be held or tossed a fair distance. It works underwater but cannot be placed on a wall or floor like Torches can. After being thrown, a Glowstick will emit light for 5 minutes and expire afterward. Glowsticks can be purchased for 10 CC each, either from the Merchant during the night (any moon phase) or from the Skeleton Merchant during the day (except for Full Moons). Glowsticks are always dropped from Blue, Pink, and Green Jellyfish, as well as from Flying Fish and Raincoat Zombies. They are also commonly found in Pots and Chests.

name: Seed
description: The Seed is a pre-Hardmode dart. While a Blowpipe or Blowgun is in a player's inventory, there is a chance that any vegetation growing on normal grass, in Clay Pots, and in Planter Boxes will drop Seeds. Flowers typically drop 4 Seeds at a time, while tall grass typically drops 1–2 Seeds.

name: Wooden Boomerang
description: The Wooden Boomerang is a pre-Hardmode boomerang that can be found in Wooden Chests located on the surface and within the underground layer. It can also be purchased from the Skeleton Merchant for 1 GC during a full moon. Its best modifier is Godly or Demonic. Both modifiers increase the average damage output by the same amount.

name: Aglet
description: The Aglet is a pre-Hardmode movement accessory that can be found in surface Chests, Wooden Crates and Pearlwood Crates. It is also sold by the Skeleton Merchant for 2 GC50 SC during a New Moon. While equipped, it increases the player's movement speed by 5%.

name: Sticky Glowstick
description: The Sticky Glowstick is a consumable light source that behaves identically to the standard Glowstick, but sticks to any surface on contact, rather than ricocheting or rolling. After being thrown, a Sticky Glowstick will emit light for 5 minutes and expire afterward. Sticky Glowsticks can be crafted, and are sometimes dropped by Pots in the Underground Snow biome.

name: Poisoned Knife
description: The Poisoned Knife is a consumable ranged weapon that has a 1/2 (50%) chance to inflict the Poisoned debuff. It otherwise behaves exactly like the Throwing Knife, except for having slightly better stats overall and auto-use. It can hit up to 2 targets.

name: Obsidian Skin Potion
description: The Obsidian Skin Potion is a buff potion which grants the Obsidian Skin buff when consumed. The buff makes the player immune to lava and the On Fire! debuff. It also grants immunity to the Burning debuff from touching Meteorite, Hellstone, Hellstone Bricks, and Ancient Hellstone Bricks for its duration. This lasts for 6 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Obsidian Skin Potions can be found in pots and Shadow Chests in the Underworld, as well as in crates.

name: Regeneration Potion
description: The Regeneration Potion is a buff potion which grants the Regeneration buff when consumed. The buff provides health regeneration by restoring an additional 2 health per second, for a total of 960 health. This lasts for 8 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Regeneration Potions can be found in Pots and Chests.

name: Swiftness Potion
description: The Swiftness Potion is a buff potion which grants the Swiftness buff when consumed. The buff increases the player's maximum movement speed by 25%, but does not stack with sprinting accessories (such as Hermes Boots), nor does it affect the speed of mounts. This lasts for 8 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Swiftness Potions can be found in pots, chests, and crates, and have a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes.

name: Gills Potion
description: The Gills Potion is a buff potion which grants the Gills buff when consumed. The buff prevents the player's breath meter from depleting and thus prevents drowning. This lasts for 4 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Gills Potions can be found in Pots, Chests, and crates.

stats.Tooltip: Allows you to breathe in liquids
stats.Buff tooltip: Allows you to breathe in liquids

name: Ironskin Potion
description: The Ironskin Potion is a buff potion that grants the Ironskin buff when consumed. The buff increases the player's defense by 8, thus reducing damage by 4 / 6 / 8. This lasts for 8 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Ironskin Potions can be found in Pots, Chests, and crates, and have a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes.

name: Mana Regeneration Potion
description: The Mana Regeneration Potion is a buff potion which grants the Mana Regeneration buff when consumed. The buff increases the rate at which the player's mana regenerates. This lasts for 8 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Mana Regeneration Potions can be found in Pots and Shadow Chests in The Underworld.

name: Magic Power Potion
description: The Magic Power Potion is a buff potion which grants the Magic Power buff when consumed. The buff increases magic damage by 20%. This lasts for 4 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Magic Power Potions can be found in Pots and Shadow Chests in The Underworld.

name: Featherfall Potion
description: The Featherfall Potion is a buff potion which grants the Featherfall buff when consumed. The buff negates fall damage, and reduces the player's fall speed and gravity to 1/3 (33.33%)[1] of normal, allowing them to jump higher and fall slower. This lasts for 10 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). While the buff is active, the player can also control their falling speed more precisely depending on the Hover Controls option in gameplay settings. The three available speeds are normal gravity, the default of 1/3rd normal gravity, and 10% of normal gravity. With the default hold control scheme, the player will experience normal gravity while the ▼ Down key is held, 10% gravity while the ▲ Up key is held, and 1/3rd gravity otherwise. With the click control scheme, their gravity will be set to 1/3rd upon touching and leaving the ground, and can be increased by one stage by pressing ▼ Down or decreased by one stage by pressing ▲ Up.

stats.Buff tooltip: Press UP or DOWN to control speed of descent

name: Spelunker Potion
description: The Spelunker Potion is a buff potion which grants the Spelunker buff when consumed. The buff illuminates valuable nearby objects and highlights them with a yellow tint. This lasts for 5 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). The Spelunker buff illuminates the following:

stats.Tooltip: Shows the location of treasure and ore
stats.Buff tooltip: Shows the location of treasure and ore

name: Invisibility Potion
description: The Invisibility Potion is a buff potion that grants the Invisibility buff when consumed. This lasts for 3 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). The buff is furthermore reduced by 1 minute if the player takes damage of any kind.Verify The buff provides the following effects:

stats.Tooltip: Grants invisibility and lowers the spawn rate of enemies

name: Shine Potion
description: The Shine Potion is a buff potion which grants the Shine buff when consumed. The buff makes the player glow brightly, which is useful for exploring caves or while exploring underwater. This lasts for 10 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Shine Potions can be found in Pots, Chests, and Crates.

name: Night Owl Potion
description: The Night Owl Potion is a buff potion that grants the Night Owl buff when consumed. The buff improves the player's night vision, and sharply increases the radius of any available light source. The buff itself has no effect in total darkness. This lasts for 10 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Night Owl Potions can be found in Pots, Chests, and Crates.

name: Battle Potion
description: The Battle Potion is a buff potion which grants the Battle buff when consumed. The buff increases the spawn rate and maximum number of enemies and critters that can be on-screen at once by 100%, effectively doubling these rates. This lasts for 7 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon ().

name: Thorns Potion
description: The Thorns Potion is a buff potion which grants the Thorns buff when consumed. The buff inflicts a knockback of 10 (Extremely strong) on enemies that come into contact with the player, and deals 100% of their damage to them.[1] This lasts for 8 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Thorns Potions can be found in Pots and Chests.

name: Water Walking Potion
description: The Water Walking Potion is a buff potion which grants the Water Walking buff when consumed. The buff allows the player to walk on the surface of all liquids (water, lava, honey, and shimmer), and prevents fall damage when falling onto liquid surfaces. The player can sink into liquids by pressing ▼ Down. This lasts for 10 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Water Walking Potions can be found in pots and chests.

stats.Tooltip: Allows the ability to walk on water

name: Archery Potion
description: The Archery Potion is a buff potion which grants the Archery buff when consumed. The buff increases the damage of arrow-firing weapons by 10%, as well as the velocity of arrows by 20%. This lasts for 8 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Archery Potions can be found in Pots and Chests.

stats.Tooltip: 10% increased bow damage and 20% increased arrow speed
stats.Buff tooltip: 10% increased bow damage and 20% increased arrow speed

name: Hunter Potion
description: The Hunter Potion is a buff potion which grants the Hunter buff when consumed. The buff shows the location of enemies, critters, and undiscovered NPCs on the screen by highlighting them in red (enemies) or green (critters and NPCs). Although these affected entities glow, they do not emit any light to the surrounding area. This lasts for 8 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Hunter Potions can be found in Pots, Chests, and Crates.

name: Gravitation Potion
description: The Gravitation Potion is a buff potion which grants the Gravitation buff when consumed. The buff allows the player to invert gravity with ▲ Up, making the screen appear upside-down and causing the player to fall upwards and walk on top of ceilings. The effect can be toggled while in midair. This lasts for 3 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). In addition to being crafted, Gravitation Potions can be found in Pots, Chests, and crates.

name: Gold Chest
description: The Gold Chest is a naturally-generated chest, with various loot as its contents. Each chest includes one special item, as well as some coins and other more common items. Gold Chests are found in Underground Cabins in the Underground and Caverns, in the Pyramids. Apart from the initial items, Gold Chests function identically to other chests. Removing all items from a Gold Chest will allow it to be freed or broken with a pickaxe or drill. It can then be picked up, placed, and used just like any other chest, with 40 slots of storage space. There are only three minor differences to other chests: Gold Chests cannot be crafted and are only generated on world creation at the random locations; they can also be sold to an NPC at 10 SC, which is more than the standard chest, which only sells for 1 SC. Gold Chests have a sparkle effect when exposed to a light source, similar to coins.

name: Daybloom Seeds
description: Daybloom Seeds are a type of seed that can be obtained from harvesting Dayblooms while they are blooming or using the Staff of Regrowth on naturally grown Dayblooms while they are fully grown. Daybloom will only bloom during daytime, showing off a sunflower-like head. Each plant will drop 1–3 Daybloom Seeds when harvested while blooming. Daybloom Seeds can be placed on pure and Hallowed grass (including Mowed grass and Mowed Hallowed grass), empty Clay Pots, or any types of Planter Boxes, and will grow into harvestable Dayblooms. They will grow anywhere, not just in direct sunlight on the Surface, but will only yield seeds when blooming.

name: Moonglow Seeds
description: Moonglow Seeds are a type of seed that can be obtained from harvesting Moonglows while they are blooming or using the Staff of Regrowth on naturally grown Moonglows while they are fully grown. Moonglow will only bloom at night, and will glow and give off blue orbs of light. Each plant drops 1–3 Moonglow Seeds when harvested while blooming. Moonglow Seeds can be placed on Jungle grass, empty Clay Pots, or any types of Planter Boxes, and will grow into harvestable Moonglows. They will grow anywhere, not just in the Jungle, but will only yield seeds when blooming.

name: Blinkroot Seeds
description: Blinkroot Seeds are a type of seed that can be obtained from harvesting Blinkroots while they are blooming or using the Staff of Regrowth on naturally grown Blinkroots while they are fully grown. A Blinkroot plant switches between its blooming and non-blooming state at random. Its blooming state can be identified by the yellow dots on the ends of its fronds and the blinking light it emits. Each plant will drop 1–3 Blinkroot Seeds when harvested while blooming. Blinkroot Seeds can be placed on Dirt and Mud Blocks, empty Clay Pots, or any types of Planter Boxes, and will grow into harvestable Blinkroots. They will grow anywhere, not just in the Underground, but will only yield seeds when blooming.

name: Deathweed Seeds
description: Deathweed Seeds are a type of seed that can be obtained from harvesting Deathweeds while they are blooming or using the Staff of Regrowth on naturally grown Deathweeds while they are fully grown. A Deathweed plant only blooms during Blood Moons and Full Moons. Its blooming state can be identified by the sickly red pods on its leaves. Each plant will drop 1–3 Deathweed Seeds when harvested while blooming. Deathweed Seeds can be placed on Corrupt and Crimson grass, Ebonstone and Crimstone Blocks, empty Clay Pots, or any types of Planter Boxes, and will grow into harvestable Deathweeds. They will grow anywhere, not just in the evil biomes, but will only yield seeds when blooming.

name: Waterleaf Seeds
description: Waterleaf Seeds are a type of seed that can be obtained from harvesting Waterleaves while they are blooming or using the Staff of Regrowth on naturally grown Waterleaves while they are fully grown. A Waterleaf plant only blooms during Rain. Each plant drops 1–3 Waterleaf Seeds when harvested while blooming. Waterleaf Seeds can be placed on Sand and Pearlsand Blocks, empty Clay Pots, or any types of Planter Boxes, even inside water or honey, and will grow into harvestable Waterleaves. They will grow anywhere, not just in the Desert, but will only yield seeds when blooming.

name: Fireblossom Seeds
description: Fireblossom Seeds are a type of seed that can be obtained from harvesting Fireblossoms while they are blooming or using the Staff of Regrowth on naturally grown Fireblossoms while they are fully grown. А Fireblossom will bloom when the sun is setting if it is not raining, from 3:45 PM to 7:30 PM. Each plant will drop 1–3 Fireblossom Seeds when harvested while blooming. Fireblossom Seeds can be placed on Ash Blocks, empty Clay Pots, or any types of Planter Boxes, even inside lava, and will grow into harvestable Fireblossoms. They will grow anywhere, not just in the Underworld, but will only yield seeds when blooming.

name: Daybloom
description: Daybloom is a type of herb that appears as a small yellow flower with a straight green stem. It grows naturally on regular grass and Hallowed grass. Daybloom can be harvested with virtually any weapon or tool, and can be planted manually using Daybloom Seeds in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes, or on suitable soil tiles. Daybloom blooms during daytime (4:30 AM to 7:29 PM). Its blooming is indicated by the yellow sunflower-like head. Harvesting mature Daybloom yields 1 Daybloom item. Harvesting blooming Daybloom yields 1 Daybloom item and 1–3 Daybloom Seeds. Harvesting blooming Daybloom with the Staff of Regrowth or Axe of Regrowth yields 1–2 Dayblooms and 1–5 Daybloom Seeds instead.

name: Moonglow
description: Moonglow is a type of herb which appears as a bent stalk with a drooping blue-white flower. It grows naturally on regular Jungle grass. Moonglow can be harvested with virtually any weapon or tool, and can be planted manually using Moonglow Seeds in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes, or on suitable soil tiles. Moonglow blooms during nighttime (7:30 PM to 4:29 AM). Its blooming is indicated by the blue flower and blue-white light particles emitted. Harvesting mature Moonglow yields 1 Moonglow item. Harvesting blooming Moonglow yields 1 Moonglow item and 1–3 Moonglow Seeds. Harvesting blooming Moonglow with the Staff of Regrowth or Axe of Regrowth yields 1–2 Moonglows and 1–5 Moonglow Seeds instead.

name: Blinkroot
description: Blinkroot is a type of herb that appears as stems with glowing dots. It grows naturally on Dirt or Mud Blocks, regardless of lighting or elevation, as long as the blocks bear no grass of any kind. Blinkroot can be harvested with virtually any weapon or tool, and can be planted manually using Blinkroot Seeds in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes, or on suitable soil tiles. Blinkroot enters and exits its bloom state randomly. Its blooming is indicated by the yellow dots and pulsating yellow light emitted. Harvesting mature Blinkroot yields 1 Blinkroot item. Harvesting blooming Blinkroot yields 1 Blinkroot item and 1–3 Blinkroot Seeds. Harvesting blooming Blinkroot with the Staff of Regrowth or Axe of Regrowth yields 1–2 Blinkroots and 1–5 Blinkroot Seeds instead.

name: Deathweed
description: Deathweed is a type of herb which appears as a short, withered purple stalk. It grows naturally on Ebonstone, Crimstone, Corrupt grass, Crimson grass, Corrupt Jungle grass, and Crimson Jungle grass. Deathweed can be harvested with virtually any weapon or tool, and can be planted manually using Deathweed Seeds in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes, or on suitable soil tiles. Deathweed blooms during a Blood Moon or a full moon at nighttime (7:30 PM to 4:29 AM). Its blooming is indicated by the pulsating purple glow. Harvesting mature Deathweed yields 1 Deathweed item. Harvesting blooming Deathweed yields 1 Deathweed item and 1–3 Deathweed Seeds. Harvesting blooming Deathweed with the Staff of Regrowth or Axe of Regrowth yields 1–2 Deathweeds and 1–5 Deathweed Seeds instead.

name: Waterleaf
description: Waterleaf is a type of herb which appears as a small, turquoise-colored sprout. It grows naturally on Sand and Pearlsand Blocks, but will never spawn in the Ocean biome.[1] Waterleaf can be harvested with virtually any weapon or tool, and can be planted manually using Waterleaf Seeds in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes, or on suitable soil tiles. Waterleaf blooms when it is raining. Its blooming is indicated by turquoise leaves and little water particles emitted. Harvesting mature Waterleaf yields 1 Waterleaf item. Harvesting blooming Waterleaf yields 1 Waterleaf item and 1–3 Waterleaf Seeds. Harvesting blooming Waterleaf with the Staff of Regrowth or Axe of Regrowth yields 1–2 Waterleafs and 1–5 Waterleaf Seeds instead.

name: Fireblossom
description: Fireblossom is a type of herb which appears as a small, magma-colored flower. It grows naturally on Ash Blocks and Ash grass. Fireblossom can be harvested with virtually any weapon or tool, and can be planted manually using Fireblossom Seeds in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes, or on suitable soil tiles. Fireblossom blooms at sunset, from 3:45 PM to 7:30 PM, unless it is raining. Its blooming is indicated by the fiery flower and fire particles emitted. Harvesting mature Fireblossom yields 1 Fireblossom item. Harvesting blooming Fireblossom yields 1 Fireblossom item and 1–3 Fireblossom Seeds. Harvesting blooming Fireblossom with the Staff of Regrowth or Axe of Regrowth yields 1–2 Fireblossoms and 1–5 Fireblossom Seeds instead.

name: Shark Fin
description: The Shark Fin is a crafting material that has a 19/20 (95%) chance of being dropped by Sharks and Orcas found in the Ocean biome, and a 1/8 (12.5%) chance of being dropped by Sand Sharks during a Sandstorm.

name: Feather
description: The Feather is a common crafting material dropped frequently by Harpies. It is used in crafting the Featherfall and Gravitation Potions and once again becomes important in Hardmode for crafting the first tier of wings – Angel and Demon Wings.

name: Tombstone
description: A Tombstone is a furniture item that drops when a player dies. It can then be retrieved and used much like a sign or as a form of decoration. By default, it contains the death message generated based on how the player died along with the real-life date of the death. Upon death, a Tombstone will bounce around until it lands on a suitable 2-tile wide surface, where it will place itself. If a Tombstone cannot find a place to land (due to lack of suitable space), it cannot be retrieved and will eventually despawn. Two Tombstones cannot land on each other.

name: Mime Mask
description: The Mime Mask is a vanity item equipped in the Social Helmet slot. It is purchased from the Clothier during a Blood Moon for 2 GC. (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) The game does not display the Mime Mask as a vanity item.

name: Antlion Mandible
description: The Antlion Mandible is a crafting material dropped by Antlions, Antlion Larvae, Antlion Chargers, and Antlion Swarmers found in the Desert and Underground Desert biomes.

name: Illegal Gun Parts
description: Illegal Gun Parts are a crafting material used to craft the Sandgun, the Megashark, and the Flamethrower. They are sold by the Arms Dealer for 20 GC at night.

name: The Doctor's Shirt
description: The Doctor's set is a vanity set which consists of The Doctor's Shirt and The Doctor's Pants. They are purchased from the Clothier for 40 GC (set) in total, during Waning Gibbous. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance.

name: The Doctor's Pants
description: The Doctor's set is a vanity set which consists of The Doctor's Shirt and The Doctor's Pants. They are purchased from the Clothier for 40 GC (set) in total, during Waning Gibbous. These items are to be equipped in the Social Slots as they only provide a visual appearance.

name: Golden Key
description: Golden Keys are required to unlock the Locked Gold Chests found in the Dungeon and Golden Lock Boxes(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) obtained by fishing in the Dungeon, as well as to free the Elder Slime(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) from an Old Shaking Chest(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). A Golden Key is consumed upon use, so each unlock will require an additional key. Golden Keys can be acquired by:

stats.Tooltip: Opens one locked Gold Chest or Lock Box (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Opens one Gold Chest (Old-gen console and 3DS versions)

name: Shadow Chest
description: The Shadow Chest is a storage item which is found only in The Underworld, generally on top of or within Ruined Houses, and contains rare items. All Shadow Chests are initially locked, requiring a Shadow Key to open. The key is not consumed when used. Each Shadow Chest contains one high-tier item of  03* rarity, along with other standard Underground loot.

name: Shadow Key
description: The Shadow Key is an item used solely to open Shadow Chests found scattered in the Underworld and Obsidian Lock Boxes found from Obsidian and Hellstone Crates. It is not consumed upon use; one Shadow Key can be used to open unlimited Shadow Chests. It can be found in Special Chests within the Dungeon (Gold Chests, Water Chests or Biome Chests), or from Golden Lock Boxes found in Dungeon and Stockade Crates.

stats.Tooltip: Opens all Shadow Chests and Obsidian Lock Boxes

name: Obsidian Brick Wall
description: The Obsidian Brick Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in Ruined Houses in the Underworld, considered unsafe. It drops as an item when destroyed, and can also be obtained via crafting. It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player.

name: Jungle Spores
description: Jungle Spores are crafting materials acquired by destroying green glowing orbs that grow randomly on Jungle grass in the Underground Jungle, in or below the Cavern layer. Each orb drops 2–3 Jungle Spores. Harvested Jungle Spores cannot be replanted. Crafting all items requires a total of (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 122 / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) 62 Jungle Spores.

name: Loom
description: The Loom is a crafting station used to craft Silk, as well as some items that require Silk as an ingredient. It can sometimes be found in Underground Cabins.

name: Piano
description: Pianos are a type of furniture. They also count as a flat surface item, which is one of the required elements in order to make a house. There is a total of (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 60 / (Old-gen console version) 15 /  10 craftable Pianos, and 5 non-craftable Pianos that can be found in the Dungeon, Ruined Houses, and Pirate Invasion event. Some Pianos can also be found in Underground Cabins, in which they follow the theme of the cabin that they are found in.

name: Dresser
description: Dressers are a type of furniture (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) as well as a storage item. It will also fulfill a house's flat-surface item requirement. There are (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 60 / (Old-gen console version) 19 / (3DS version) 5 craftable versions, and (Desktop, Console, Old-gen console and Mobile versions) 5 noncraftable versions which must be looted from various locations in the world.

name: Bench
description: Benches are decorative furniture items that can take the place of chairs for the comfort requirement in NPCs' housing. As with all furniture that can be sat on, sitting on any Bench will increase the player's Fishing Power by 5.

name: Bathtub
description: The Bathtub is a decorative furniture item. It fulfills a house's requirement of a flat-surface item. There is a total of (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 65 / (Old-gen console version) 16 / (3DS version) 11 Bathtub types, all being functionally identical but presenting different appearances.

name: Red Banner
description: Banners are decorative furniture items that can be placed on the underside of blocks. Many are found naturally-placed in various biomes. There are also (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) four / (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) six crafted banners (Red, Green, Blue, Yellow, (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Pink, White) using Silk. Banners can be hung off of blocks and off of platforms which are in (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) any position / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) their bottom position (after being struck three times with a hammer). Faction flags are banners found in the Dungeon. In post-Plantera worlds, the faction flags found hanging in a particular Dungeon area correspond to the background wall type (brick, slab, or tile), both of which signify the types of enemies that will spawn there. See Hardmode Dungeon for more info.

name: Green Banner
description: Banners are decorative furniture items that can be placed on the underside of blocks. Many are found naturally-placed in various biomes. There are also (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) four / (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) six crafted banners (Red, Green, Blue, Yellow, (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Pink, White) using Silk. Banners can be hung off of blocks and off of platforms which are in (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) any position / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) their bottom position (after being struck three times with a hammer). Faction flags are banners found in the Dungeon. In post-Plantera worlds, the faction flags found hanging in a particular Dungeon area correspond to the background wall type (brick, slab, or tile), both of which signify the types of enemies that will spawn there. See Hardmode Dungeon for more info.

name: Blue Banner
description: Banners are decorative furniture items that can be placed on the underside of blocks. Many are found naturally-placed in various biomes. There are also (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) four / (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) six crafted banners (Red, Green, Blue, Yellow, (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Pink, White) using Silk. Banners can be hung off of blocks and off of platforms which are in (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) any position / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) their bottom position (after being struck three times with a hammer). Faction flags are banners found in the Dungeon. In post-Plantera worlds, the faction flags found hanging in a particular Dungeon area correspond to the background wall type (brick, slab, or tile), both of which signify the types of enemies that will spawn there. See Hardmode Dungeon for more info.

name: Yellow Banner
description: Banners are decorative furniture items that can be placed on the underside of blocks. Many are found naturally-placed in various biomes. There are also (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) four / (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) six crafted banners (Red, Green, Blue, Yellow, (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Pink, White) using Silk. Banners can be hung off of blocks and off of platforms which are in (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) any position / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) their bottom position (after being struck three times with a hammer). Faction flags are banners found in the Dungeon. In post-Plantera worlds, the faction flags found hanging in a particular Dungeon area correspond to the background wall type (brick, slab, or tile), both of which signify the types of enemies that will spawn there. See Hardmode Dungeon for more info.

name: Lamp Post
description: The Lamp Post is a piece of furniture that emits light. It can be placed underwater and can be toggled with wires. It counts as a light source for housing.

name: Tiki Torch
description: The Tiki Torch is a stationary light source that must be placed in order to provide light. Like Torches, it can be placed on platforms, and will be put out, broken and subsequently dropped when coming into contact with water. However, the Tiki Torch cannot be collected by pressing the ⚷ Open / Activate button on it.

name: Barrel
description: The Barrel is a storage item that has 40 slots for storing items, similar to a Chest. The crafting recipe requires only half as much metal as for a Chest, but requires a Sawmill rather than a Work Bench. Other than this, the difference is purely aesthetic. Barrels can only be harvested with a pickaxe or drill when empty.

name: Chinese Lantern
description: Lanterns are small light sources that hang from ceilings (the underside of blocks). They occupy less room than a chandelier, making them ideal for smaller spaces. Most lanterns can be toggled on and off via wire. There are two lanterns that provide regeneration buffs, the Heart Lantern and the Star in a Bottle, which grant health and mana regeneration, respectively. Toggling these lanterns off will only deactivate the light source, but not the buff.

name: Cooking Pot
description: The Cooking Pot is a crafting station that is solely used to turn various items into food, consumables that induce the Well Fed, Plenty Satisfied and Exquisitely Stuffed buffs. Such food material include the Bowl of Soup, Cooked Fish, Cooked Shrimp, and Grub Soup. The Cauldron is the Halloween-themed variant of the Cooking Pot that is functionally identical in every way, except for its animated sprite and the small amount of green light it emits. Unlike the Cooking Pot, the Cauldron cannot be crafted, and can only be purchased from the Witch Doctor for 1 GC50 SC during Halloween.

name: Safe
description: The Safe is a portable storage item with 40 slots to store other items. The Safe, Piggy Bank, Defender's Forge(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), and Void Vault(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) have the unique ability to be carried while they contain items. The Safe can be purchased from the Merchant for 20 GC after Skeletron has been defeated. The Safe functions identically to the Piggy Bank. However, it can also be placed on blocks in addition to platforms or on other flat furniture pieces. Additionally, the Safe and Piggy Bank do not share storage space.

stats.Tooltip: Can be used to store your itemsStored items can only be accessed by youStored coins can be used in shops

name: Skull Lantern
description: The Skull Lantern is a decorative piece of Furniture that provides light. They always face left, and cannot be turned off via wires and a switch.

name: Trash Can
description: The Trash Can is a storage item which has 40 storage slots. They essentially serve the same purpose as Chests, only having the appearance of a trash can. Trash Cans can only be harvested when empty. When given a signal, they collect nearby dropped items in a 192 pixel radius. [1]

name: Candelabra
description: Candelabras are a type of furniture and light source that emits light when placed on a flat surface such as blocks, platforms, and flat furniture items. It is not destroyed by water. There are (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 61 / (Old-gen console version) 23 /  12 craftable and 5 looted variations of the Candelabra.

name: Pink Vase
description: The Pink Vase is a decorative furniture item. A placed Pink Vase also serves as a Placed Bottle for potion crafting.

name: Mug
description: The Mug is a cup made of Glass. It can be used to craft Ale. It can be placed on a flat furniture item, where it functions as a Placed Bottle crafting station.

name: Keg
description: The Keg is a crafting station used to brew Ale. In addition to being crafted, it can sometimes be found in Underground Cabins.

name: Ale
description:
Ale is a potion that grants the Tipsy debuff for 2 minutes when consumed. Ale can be crafted at a Keg or purchased from the Tavernkeep(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) for 1 SC each. The Tipsy debuff provides the following effects:
This content is transcluded from Tipsy.

stats.Tooltip: Minor improvements to melee stats & lowered defense'Down the hatch!'

name: Bookcase
description: Bookcases are a type of furniture and crafting station used for the crafting of three Hardmode magic weapons (the Crystal Storm, Cursed Flames, and Golden Shower) as well as the Librarian furniture out of Librarian Blocks. Bookcases also fulfill a house's flat surface item requirement. There is a total of 65 types of Bookcases: 60 craftable Bookcases, and 5 looted from the environment: one from the Underworld, three from the Dungeon, and one from the Pirate Invasion. Some bookcases can also be found in Underground Cabins; their style follows the theme of the cabin where they are found.

name: Throne
description: The Throne is a decorative furniture item. It fulfills the comfort item requirement for the purposes of NPC housing. As with all furniture that can be sat on, sitting on the Throne will increase the player's Fishing Power by 5.

name: Bowl
description: Bowls are a type of decorative furniture that can be placed on flat-surface items. Unlike plates, food cannot be placed in bowls. Only the regular Bowl can be used in crafting. The regular Bowl can also occasionally be found in the Dungeon on top of Tables and Work Benches.

name: Bowl of Soup
description: The Bowl of Soup is a food item crafted at the Cooking Pot. When consumed, it grants the Plenty Satisfied buff. This lasts for 8 minutes, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). Like all other food items, the Bowl of Soup can be placed on a Plate:

stats.Tooltip: Medium improvements to all stats'Simple, yet refreshing.'
stats.Buff: Plenty Satisfied(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions)

name: Toilet
description: Toilets are furniture items which can be placed on the ground. They are functionally similar to chairs, and count as comfort items for NPC house requirements. Similarly to chairs, Toilets can be sat on by pressing the ⚷ Open / Activate button on them. Having any tier of Well Fed active while sitting on a Toilet allows the production of Poo. As with all furniture that can be sat on, sitting on any Toilet will increase the player's Fishing Power by 5.[1]

stats.Tooltip: 'How desperate are you?' (Cactus)'Seriously? THIS is what you used the Broken Hero Sword for?' (Terra)

name: Grandfather Clock
description: Grandfather Clocks are furniture items which provide information about the in-game time. If a player is nearby a Grandfather Clock (about 80 tiles horizontally and 50 tiles vertically), the time is displayed on the right side of the screen, underneath the minimap. The time is rounded to the last hour, similar to Tin and Copper Watches, but pressing ⚷ Open / Activate on it will show precise time in minutes as a status message on the bottom left corner. There are (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 61 / (Old-gen console version) 21 /  5 craftable versions and (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 5 / (Old-gen console version) 2 noncraftable (but still lootable) versions. The regular Grandfather Clock can rarely be found in Underground Cabins. All are functionally identical, and differ only in their appearance.

name: Armor Statue
description: Statues are furniture items. While some are purely decorative, others have a functional effect when connected to wires and activated: Depending on the statue, upon activation it may spawn enemies, drop items, or teleport an NPC to the statue's location. Most statues can be found already placed in any area underground (most frequently in the Dungeon, Underground Cabins, and Cavern level), though Angel Statues also appear in Chests. Like all furniture, a pickaxe, a drill, or explosives are used to collect them. There are a total of 117 different statues: 25 decoration statues, 36 text and numerical statues, and 56 functional mechanisms.

name: Goblin Battle Standard
description: The Goblin Battle Standard is an event-summoning item that initiates the Goblin Army event when used. The Goblin Battle Standard is consumed when used, so one must be crafted for each invasion the player wishes to summon. In order for it to be used, at least one player in the current world must also have 200 health or more. The Tattered Cloth required for crafting the Goblin Battle Standard is obtained by killing Goblin Scouts, which spawn occasionally in the East and West sixths of the world.

name: Tattered Cloth
description: The Tattered Cloth is a crafting material dropped by Goblin Scouts.

name: Sawmill
description: The Sawmill is a crafting station for advanced Wood and Furniture crafting. It is 3 blocks wide, 3 blocks high, and must be placed on top of solid blocks or platforms.

stats.Tooltip: Used for crafting advanced items such as beds, looms and dressers

name: Cobalt Ore
description: Cobalt Ore belongs to the first tier of the early Hardmode ores. Upon the first altar broken, either it or Palladium Ore can spawn in the underground and cavern layers. Mining Cobalt Ore requires a Molten Pickaxe or better. As with all early Hardmode ores, decreasing quantities of the ore spawn with every third altar broken afterwards, it is immune to explosives (apart from the Power Bomb and Sticky Power Bomb), and it can be obtained from Hardmode crates.

name: Mythril Ore
description: Mythril Ore belongs to the second tier of the early Hardmode ores. Upon the second altar broken, either it or Orichalcum Ore can spawn in the cavern layer. Mining Mythril Ore requires a Cobalt/Palladium Pickaxe or better. As with all early Hardmode ores, decreasing quantities of the ore spawn with every third altar broken afterwards, it is immune to explosives (apart from the Power Bomb and Sticky Power Bomb), and it can be obtained from Hardmode crates.

name: Adamantite Ore
description: Adamantite Ore belongs to the third tier of the early Hardmode ores. Upon the third altar broken, either it or Titanium Ore can spawn in the cavern layer, roughly at lava depth. Mining Adamantite Ore requires a Mythril/Orichalcum Pickaxe or better. As with all early Hardmode ores, decreasing quantities of the ore spawn with every third altar broken afterwards, it is immune to explosives (apart from the Power Bomb and Sticky Power Bomb), and it can be obtained from Hardmode crates.

name: Pwnhammer
description: The Pwnhammer is a Hardmode hammer that is always dropped by the Wall of Flesh. It is able to destroy Demon Altars and Crimson Altars, causing one type of Hardmode ore to appear in the world. Its best modifier is Legendary. However, due to rounding, Legendary, Light, Deadly, Agile, Quick, and Nasty provide equal boosts to mining speed for this tool.

stats.Tooltip: Strong enough to destroy Demon Altars

name: Excalibur
description: The Excalibur is a Hardmode, post-one mechanical boss broadsword. When swung, a large yellow aura follows the swing, reaching about twice the distance of the sword itself. The aura can hit up to three enemies. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Hallowed Seeds
description: Hallowed Seeds are Hardmode seeds used to plant Hallowed grass on Dirt Blocks, which will then spread The Hallow. Hallowed Seeds are purchased from the Dryad in Hardmode for 20 SC each. Hallowed Seeds can be used by the Rubblemaker to turn into various placeable objects:

name: Ebonsand Block
description: Sand Blocks are soil blocks found in vast quantities in Desert biomes, at shore and bottom of Ocean biomes, and can be found as microbiomes in patches in the Underground layer. They are also shot as projectiles by Antlions. Sand can spread and be converted by the evil biomes and The Hallow (see below). Sand is subject to gravity, a unique quality only shared by Slush, Silt, Shell Piles, Cracked Dungeon Bricks, and Ash on For the Worthy and Zenith worlds. Sand Blocks will deal damage to any player or enemy they fall on. If the amount of Sand falling on the player is significant, the Suffocation debuff will be inflicted until the player manages to escape. Sand is used to craft a small number of building blocks and other items directly, but it is also the raw material for Glass, which is used for a full furniture set and a variety of other items.

stats.Tooltip: Will suffocate you if it falls on you
stats.Duration: Until the player gets out from under the Sand Blocks

name: Cobalt Hat
description: Cobalt armor is a Hardmode armor set consisting of the Cobalt Breastplate and Cobalt Leggings, as well as three different headpieces: the Cobalt Helmet, Cobalt Mask, and Cobalt Hat, providing bonuses for the melee, ranged, and magic damage type respectively. Crafting a full set with one headpiece requires a total of 45 Cobalt Bars (135 Cobalt Ore). Crafting all five pieces requires a total of 65 Cobalt Bars (195 Cobalt Ore). Its alternate ore counterpart is the Palladium armor.

stats.Set Bonus: : 14% reduced mana costs : 15% increased melee speed : 20% chance to save ammo
stats.Tooltip: Increases maximum mana by 4010% increased magic damage9% increased magic critical strike chance

name: Cobalt Bar
description: The Cobalt Bar is a Hardmode bar. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Palladium Bar.

name: Mythril Bar
description: The Mythril Bar is a Hardmode bar. It is required to create a Hardmode Anvil, which is used to craft most Hardmode equipment. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Orichalcum Bar.

name: Cobalt Chainsaw
description: The Cobalt Chainsaw is a Hardmode chainsaw and the alternative to the Cobalt Waraxe. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Palladium Chainsaw.

name: Mythril Chainsaw
description: The Mythril Chainsaw is a Hardmode chainsaw and the alternative to the Mythril Waraxe. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Orichalcum Chainsaw.

stats.Use time: 15 (Very fast) (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) /8 (Insanely fast) (Old-gen console and 3DS versions)

name: Cobalt Drill
description: The Cobalt Drill is a Hardmode drill. It is the alternative to the Cobalt Pickaxe, having a faster mining speed but shorter range and lacking the ability to benefit from mining speed bonuses. It can mine blocks up to Mythril and Orichalcum Ore. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Palladium Drill. The Cobalt Drill has slightly less pickaxe power and damage.

name: Mythril Drill
description: The Mythril Drill is a Hardmode drill. It is the alternative to the Mythril Pickaxe, having a faster mining speed but shorter range and lacking the ability to benefit from mining speed bonuses. It can mine blocks up to Adamantite and Titanium Ore. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Orichalcum Drill. The Mythril Drill has slightly less pickaxe power, damage, and mining speed.

name: Adamantite Chainsaw
description: The Adamantite Chainsaw is a Hardmode chainsaw and the alternative to the Adamantite Waraxe. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Titanium Chainsaw.

name: Adamantite Drill
description: The Adamantite Drill is a Hardmode drill. It is the alternative to the Adamantite Pickaxe, having a faster mining speed but shorter range and lacking the ability to benefit from mining speed bonuses. It can mine blocks up to Adamantite and Titanium Ore. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Titanium Drill. The Adamantite Drill has slightly more pickaxe power and damage.

stats.Knockback: 0.5 (Extremely weak) (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) / 0 (No knockback) (Old-gen console and 3DS versions)

name: Dao of Pow
description: The Dao of Pow is a Hardmode flail. It has a 4/5 (80%) chance of inflicting the Confused debuff for 2–4 seconds upon striking an enemy. Its best modifier is Godly or Demonic. Both modifiers increase the average damage output by the same amount.

stats.Tooltip: Has a chance to confuse'Find your inner pieces'

name: Mythril Halberd
description: The Mythril Halberd is a Hardmode spear. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Orichalcum Halberd.

name: Adamantite Bar
description: The Adamantite Bar is a Hardmode bar. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Titanium Bar.

name: Glass Wall
description: Glass Walls are transparent walls that can be used to make windows that let in natural light, while preventing monster spawns. They are crafted from Glass and also spawn naturally in houses on Floating Islands. They can be crafted into Stained Glass.

name: Compass
description: The Compass is an informational accessory that enables an on-screen display of the player's horizontal position. Position is shown in the number of feet (1 foot = 1/2 tile) to the east or west of the center of the map. The centerpoint of the map is usually very close to the initial spawn point. The Compass has a 79/6400 (1.23%) chance of being dropped by Crawdads, Giant Shellies, and Salamanders, a 1/75 (1.33%) chance to be dropped by Piranhas, and a 1/100 (1%) chance to be dropped by Mother Slimes, Snow Flinx, Undead Vikings, and Armored Vikings.

name: Diving Gear
description: The Diving Gear is an accessory that combines the functionality of the Flipper and Diving Helmet:

stats.Tooltip: Grants the ability to swimGreatly extends underwater breathing

name: GPS
description: The GPS is an informational accessory that enables an on-screen display of the in-game time, the player's horizontal position, and their depth.

name: Obsidian Horseshoe
description: The Obsidian Horseshoe is a pre-Hardmode immunity accessory that grants the following effects while equipped:

stats.Tooltip: Negates fall damageGrants immunity to fire blocks

name: Obsidian Shield
description: The Obsidian Shield is a pre-Hardmode shield and immunity accessory that grants +2 defense, immunity to knockback, and immunity to the Burning debuff.

stats.Tooltip: Grants immunity to knockbackGrants immunity to fire blocks

name: Tinkerer's Workshop
description: The Tinkerer's Workshop is a crafting station that is used primarily to combine multiple accessories into single items that usually provide the abilities of their components. For instance, it can combine the Rocket Boots and Hermes Boots into a single item (the Spectre Boots) that provides the same rocket-jumping and accelerated movement as the individual items do, without occupying two accessory slots. The Tinkerer's Workshop can be purchased from the Goblin Tinkerer for 10 GC. (Desktop, Console, Old-gen console and Mobile versions) When combining items, the Tinkerer's Workshop will assign a new random modifier to the crafted item. The modifiers the individual items had beforehand do not influence the modifier of the new combined item.

stats.Tooltip: Allows the combining of some accessories

name: Cloud in a Balloon
description: The Cloud in a Balloon is an accessory that combines the functionality of the Cloud in a Bottle and the Shiny Red Balloon. It boosts jump duration by 33% and jump speed by 30%, increasing the player's first jump's height to 10.5 tiles (~75% increase), and provides a double jump that can reach a height of 9 tiles, for a total height of 19.5 tiles. The total jump height of the Cloud in a Balloon is reduced when underwater, reducing the player's total underwater jump height from 15 tiles (8 + 7 from Cloud jump) to 13 tiles (7 + 6 from Cloud jump).

stats.Tooltip: Allows the holder to double jumpIncreases jump height

name: Adamantite Headgear
description: Adamantite armor is a Hardmode armor set consisting of the Adamantite Breastplate and Adamantite Leggings, as well as three different headpieces: the Adamantite Helmet, Adamantite Mask, and Adamantite Headgear, providing bonuses for the melee, ranged, and magic damage type respectively. Crafting a full set with one headpiece requires a total of 54 Adamantite Bars (216 Adamantite Ore). Crafting all five pieces requires a total of 78 Adamantite Bars (312 Adamantite Ore). Its alternate ore counterpart is the Titanium armor.

stats.Set Bonus: : 19% reduced mana costs : 20% increased melee and movement speed : 25% chance to save ammo
stats.Tooltip: 14% increased ranged damage10% increased ranged critical strike chance

name: Spectre Boots
description: The Spectre Boots are a pre-Hardmode accessory crafted from Rocket Boots and either Hermes Boots, Flurry Boots, Sailfish Boots(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), or Dunerider Boots(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions), and combines their functionality (except the faster speed on sand from Dunerider Boots). They grant the following effects while equipped:

stats.Tooltip: Allows flightThe wearer can run super fast

name: Adamantite Glaive
description: The Adamantite Glaive is a Hardmode spear. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Titanium Trident.

name: Toolbelt
description: The Toolbelt is a construction accessory that can be purchased from the Goblin Tinkerer for 10 GC. While equipped, it grants +1 placement range, but not tool range.

name: Pearlsand Block
description: Sand Blocks are soil blocks found in vast quantities in Desert biomes, at shore and bottom of Ocean biomes, and can be found as microbiomes in patches in the Underground layer. They are also shot as projectiles by Antlions. Sand can spread and be converted by the evil biomes and The Hallow (see below). Sand is subject to gravity, a unique quality only shared by Slush, Silt, Shell Piles, Cracked Dungeon Bricks, and Ash on For the Worthy and Zenith worlds. Sand Blocks will deal damage to any player or enemy they fall on. If the amount of Sand falling on the player is significant, the Suffocation debuff will be inflicted until the player manages to escape. Sand is used to craft a small number of building blocks and other items directly, but it is also the raw material for Glass, which is used for a full furniture set and a variety of other items.

stats.Tooltip: Will suffocate you if it falls on you
stats.Duration: Until the player gets out from under the Sand Blocks

name: Pearlstone Block
description: The Pearlstone Block is a Hardmode block type found in The Hallow. It requires at least a Nightmare Pickaxe, Deathbringer Pickaxe, or explosives to mine. Pearlstone is the Hallow version of Stone (similar to Ebonstone in The Corruption, or Crimstone in The Crimson). In a way similar to Ebonstone and Crimstone, Pearlstone can be converted into ordinary Stone via Vile Powder, Vicious Powder, or the Clentaminator with the Green Solution.

name: Mining Shirt
description: Mining armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Mining Helmet, Mining Shirt, and Mining Pants. The Mining Helmet can be purchased from the Merchant for 4 GC and can also be dropped by Undead Miners with a 1/20 (5%) chance. The Mining Shirt and Mining Pants are dropped from Undead Miners with a 3/25 (12%) chance. The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

name: Mining Pants
description: Mining armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Mining Helmet, Mining Shirt, and Mining Pants. The Mining Helmet can be purchased from the Merchant for 4 GC and can also be dropped by Undead Miners with a 1/20 (5%) chance. The Mining Shirt and Mining Pants are dropped from Undead Miners with a 3/25 (12%) chance. The full set grants the following effects while equipped:

name: Pearlstone Brick
description: Pearlstone Brick is a brick made from Pearlstone. It does not spread The Hallow when placed, therefore making it a safer construction material than its raw ingredients.

name: Iridescent Brick
description: Iridescent Brick is a cheap brick. It can be mined with any pickaxe or drill. It and its walls can either be crafted, or collected from Jungle Shrines in the Underground Jungle.

name: Mudstone Brick
description: The Mudstone Brick is a type of brick made from Stone Blocks and Mud Blocks. It can also be obtained from Jungle Shrines. It is brown in color with a Stone-like texture.

name: Cobalt Brick
description: Cobalt Brick is a сobalt tier brick.

name: Mythril Brick
description: Mythril Brick is a mythril tier brick.

name: Pearlstone Brick Wall
description: Pearlstone Brick Walls are a type of wall crafted from Pearlstone Bricks. Like most walls, they will stop enemy spawning, and can be used for NPC houses.

name: Iridescent Brick Wall
description: The Iridescent Brick Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in Jungle Shrines in some worlds. It drops as an item when destroyed, and can also be obtained via crafting. It is always considered a safe wall. Iridescent Brick Walls reflect the color of the light that they are lit with.

name: Mudstone Brick Wall
description: The Mudstone Brick Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in Jungle Shrines in some worlds. It drops as an item when destroyed, and can also be obtained via crafting. It is always considered a safe wall.

name: Cobalt Brick Wall
description: Cobalt Brick Wall is a background wall crafted from Cobalt Bricks. es-formal:Pared de ladrillo de cobalto

name: Mythril Brick Wall
description: Mythril Brick Wall is a background wall crafted from Mythril Bricks.

name: Holy Water
description: Thrown Water refers to a set of three consumable weapons that each convert a small area into a biome (Hallow, Corruption, or Crimson for Holy Water, Unholy Water, and Blood Water respectively) when thrown. This conversion affects a roughly circular area with a radius of four tiles from the center. Enemies within this radius are also damaged slightly; this damage is not classified as any of the four damage types and therefore cannot be modified by any buffs or equipment. Holy Water is only available in Hardmode. In contrast, Unholy and Blood Water are available in pre-Hardmode, since the Ebonsand or Crimsand required for crafting them can be obtained by using Vile/Vicious Powder to convert pure sand.

stats.Tooltip: Spreads the Crimson to some blocks

name: Unholy Water
description: Thrown Water refers to a set of three consumable weapons that each convert a small area into a biome (Hallow, Corruption, or Crimson for Holy Water, Unholy Water, and Blood Water respectively) when thrown. This conversion affects a roughly circular area with a radius of four tiles from the center. Enemies within this radius are also damaged slightly; this damage is not classified as any of the four damage types and therefore cannot be modified by any buffs or equipment. Holy Water is only available in Hardmode. In contrast, Unholy and Blood Water are available in pre-Hardmode, since the Ebonsand or Crimsand required for crafting them can be obtained by using Vile/Vicious Powder to convert pure sand.

stats.Tooltip: Spreads the Crimson to some blocks

name: Silt Block
description: Silt Blocks are a type of soil blocks that generates in the Underground and Cavern layers, appearing more frequently at lower depths. Silt is affected by gravity and will deal damage to any player or enemy it falls on. Unlike Sand Blocks, silt can stop the Hallow, the Crimson, and the Corruption from spreading. Silt looks similar to Ash Blocks, but is a lighter color. Silt can be placed in an Extractinator or Chlorophyte Extractinator to be converted into various valuable materials, including gems, coins, ores, and very rarely, the Amber Mosquito pet summoning item.

stats.Tooltip: Will suffocate you if it falls on youCan be extractinated

name: Fairy Bell
description: The Fairy Bell is a Hardmode light pet-summoning item that spawns a Fairy, a glowing creature resembling a Pixie, which follows the player and produces light for an unlimited duration. The summoned Fairy will be either pink, green, or blue, the color of which changes randomly each time the Fairy is summoned. The Fairy is faster and brighter than the Shadow Orb and Crimson Heart, but not as fast or bright as the Wisp in a Bottle.

name: Breaker Blade
description: The Breaker Blade is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/4 (25%) chance of being dropped by the Wall of Flesh. It has a larger size than most other swords. It deals 250% damage (175 base damage) to enemies over 90% health.[1] Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Deals more damage to unhurt enemies

name: Blue Torch
description: Torches are furniture items that function as light sources when placed, held, or dropped (via the Throw button). Regular Torches can be found randomly in Chests and Pots, as a bonus drop from slimes, or purchased from the Merchant or Skeleton Merchant for 50 CC each. Placing at least 101 Torches in close proximity below the surface layer in any biome will start the Torch God mini-event. Completing the event will grant the Torch God's Favor, which allows the player to automatically convert ordinary Torches to one of the biome types.

name: Clockwork Assault Rifle
description: The Clockwork Assault Rifle is a Hardmode gun that has a 1/4 (25%) chance of being dropped by the Wall of Flesh. When used, it fires in three-shot bursts, with each burst consuming one piece of ammunition. Its best modifier is Demonic, as it does not have any knockback and thus cannot get modifiers that affect it.

stats.Tooltip: Three round burstOnly the first shot consumes ammo

name: Cobalt Repeater
description: The Cobalt Repeater is a Hardmode repeater. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Palladium Repeater.

name: Mythril Repeater
description: The Mythril Repeater is a Hardmode repeater. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Orichalcum Repeater.

name: Dual Hook
description: The Dual Hook is a Hardmode grappling hook that reaches 27.5 tiles and has two hooks which can be launched simultaneously; however, only one hook can be attached to a block at any time. Launching a second hook will not release the player from the first hook unless the new hook successfully attaches to a block. The Dual Hook has a 1/6 (16.67%) chance of being dropped by Mimics.

name: Star Statue
description: Statues are furniture items. While some are purely decorative, others have a functional effect when connected to wires and activated: Depending on the statue, upon activation it may spawn enemies, drop items, or teleport an NPC to the statue's location. Most statues can be found already placed in any area underground (most frequently in the Dungeon, Underground Cabins, and Cavern level), though Angel Statues also appear in Chests. Like all furniture, a pickaxe, a drill, or explosives are used to collect them. There are a total of 117 different statues: 25 decoration statues, 36 text and numerical statues, and 56 functional mechanisms.

There are a total of 117 different statues: 25 decoration statues, 36 text and numerical statues, and 56 functional mechanisms.

name: Planked Wall
description: Planked Wall is a background wall that naturally spawns in Underground Cabins. They can be crafted at a Sawmill with one Stone Block and one Wood.

name: Wooden Beam
description: Beams or columns are background furniture items that provide support for Torches and can be used as decoration. They form continuous vertical pillars when stacked. They are found naturally in Underground Cabins, and can be crafted. Beams and columns can be stacked from the ground, and exist in the background, providing support for suspended Torches that will not inhibit character movement. In this way, beams and columns provide a function similar to trees (which also exist in the background and allow the player to suspend high Torches), but which the player can construct where they like. Where background walls exist, beams and columns can also be placed in "midair", without support from below.

name: Adamantite Repeater
description: The Adamantite Repeater is a Hardmode repeater. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Titanium Repeater.

name: Adamantite Sword
description: The Adamantite Sword is a Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Titanium Sword.

name: Cobalt Sword
description: The Cobalt Sword is a Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Palladium Sword.

name: Mythril Sword
description: The Mythril Sword is a Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Orichalcum Sword.

name: Moon Charm
description: The Moon Charm is a Hardmode accessory that, when equipped during nighttime, transforms the player into a werewolf and applies the Werewolf buff, which provides several stat bonuses. Additionally, the Moon Charm replaces the sound the player makes when damaged with the hurt sound that regular Werewolf enemies also have. The Werewolf buff does not pacify Werewolf enemies or make the player immune to damage from them. Although the regular Werewolf enemy is colored blue, the player Werewolf will be colored brown. The Moon Charm has a 1/60 (1.67%) chance of being dropped by Werewolves. The Werewolf buff provides the player with these bonuses:[1]

stats.Tooltip: Turns the holder into a werewolf at night
stats.Duration: From 7:30 PM to 4:30 AM while equipped

name: Ruler
description: The Ruler is a pre-Hardmode shortsword purchased from the Goblin Tinkerer for 10 SC. It swings four times in quick succession per use, with slightly varying angles with each stab. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Crystal Ball
description: The Crystal Ball is a Hardmode furniture item which functions as a crafting station. It is purchased from the Wizard for 10 GC. Pressing ⚷ Open / Activate on a placed Crystal Ball grants the Clairvoyance buff and opens its crafting menu. The Clairvoyance buff boosts the following magic-related stats:

stats.Tooltip: Usable when placedMagic powers are increased

name: Disco Ball
description: The Disco Ball is a novelty Hardmode light source that can be placed on the underside of blocks, generating short pulses of colored light that cycle through the spectrum (In the Nintendo 3DS version, the Disco Ball will cycle through red, green, and blue only). The light can be toggled with wires. It is purchased from the Merchant in Hardmode for 1 GC each.

name: Sorcerer Emblem
description: The Sorcerer Emblem is a Hardmode accessory that increases magic damage by 15%. It has a 1/4 (25%) chance to drop from the Wall of Flesh.

name: Warrior Emblem
description: The Warrior Emblem is a Hardmode accessory that increases melee damage by 15%. It has a 1/4 (25%) chance to drop from the Wall of Flesh.

name: Ranger Emblem
description: The Ranger Emblem is a Hardmode accessory that increases ranged damage by 15%. It has a 1/4 (25%) chance to drop from the Wall of Flesh.

name: Demon Wings
description: Wings are accessories that allow the player to fly temporarily by holding the ↷ Jump button. They also provide immunity to fall damage. After flight time has run out, holding the ↷ Jump button allows the player to glide, slowing falling speed. Flight time resets when the player stands, climbs (e.g. using Rope or Shoe Spikes), or hooks onto a block. Flight is much more controlled with wings than with Rocket Boots and the like, and they allow for much greater flight range. The player can only equip one set of wings at a time. Equipping wings generally replaces jumps entirely with flight, rather than requiring a double-jump to activate. Extra jump accessories like the Cloud in a Bottle will still grant an additional jump, which can be used to instantly boost the wing to full speed upon reactivation in midair.

stats.Tooltip: Can be activated with the right materials'At every setback, begin again... wiser.'

name: Angel Wings
description: Wings are accessories that allow the player to fly temporarily by holding the ↷ Jump button. They also provide immunity to fall damage. After flight time has run out, holding the ↷ Jump button allows the player to glide, slowing falling speed. Flight time resets when the player stands, climbs (e.g. using Rope or Shoe Spikes), or hooks onto a block. Flight is much more controlled with wings than with Rocket Boots and the like, and they allow for much greater flight range. The player can only equip one set of wings at a time. stats.Tooltip: Can be activated with the right materials'At every setback, begin again... wiser.'

name: Magical Harp
description: The Magical Harp is a Hardmode magic weapon that autofires ricocheting, piercing projectiles that graphically resemble purple musical notes. Each projectile lasts for about four seconds before dissipating and will both ricochet and pierce enemies an unlimited number of times until this time limit is reached. The Magical Harp's musical notes gain a 5% damage penalty for each enemy hit. Notes produce a reasonable amount of purple light and are not affected by gravity. The speed of each note can be controlled by clicking closer or further away from the character: Closer clicks fire slower-moving notes, and further clicks fire faster notes. The speed at which each note travels does not affect its lifespan; all last about four seconds. The Magical Harp also plays sound while firing that resembles plucked strings, with a pitch that correlates to the weapon's firing speed: firing slowly produces lower-pitched sounds, and faster firings produce higher-pitched sounds.

name: Rainbow Rod
description: The Rainbow Rod is a Hardmode, post-The Twins wand. When used, it fires a rainbow missile that follows the cursor when ⚒ Use / Attack key is held. If the ⚒ Use / Attack key is released mid-drag, the missile will be fired in that direction. If the cursor is stationary on release, it is fired directly away from the player. Once it is released, the missile homes in on nearby enemies and can pierce twice, creating a small explosion upon contact with a block or third enemy. The missile does not dissipate when touching a block while being controlled, and is not affected by liquids (unlike the Flamelash). Its best modifier is Mythical.

name: Ice Rod
description: The Ice Rod is a Hardmode magic weapon that autofires blocks of ice, which have the unique ability of placing solid, temporary  Ice Blocks at the location of the cursor and which last for 15 seconds before shattering. These Ice Blocks are translucent, cast a dim, flickering blue light, and are slippery like normal Ice Blocks. The Ice Rod can be purchased from the Wizard for 50 GC. The location of the cursor must be unoccupied in order to form an Ice Block. If the tile is occupied either by blocks or background objects, the fired Ice Block acts as an ordinary projectile, flying in a straight line and unaffected by gravity, and shatters when it hits an enemy or other foreground objects. If the cursor is on an existing Ice Block from a previous casting, it will cause the next projectile to pass through the existing Ice Block.

name: Neptune's Shell
description: Neptune's Shell is a Hardmode accessory that aids maneuverability in water, honey, and shimmer by granting indefinite breathing time while submerged, swimming via the ↷ Jump key, and eliminating the slow movement speed usually caused by submerging underwater. Entering applicable liquids with this accessory equipped transforms the player into a merperson and grants the Merfolk buff. While in this form, the breath meter will refill as if the player is in open air, therefore effectively preventing drowning. Neptune's Shell has a 1/50 (2%) / 99/2500 (3.96%) chance of being dropped by Creatures from the Deep.

stats.Tooltip: Transforms the holder into merfolk when entering water
stats.Buff tooltip: Can breathe and move easily underwater

name: Mannequin
description: The Mannequin is a decorative furniture item that can be used to display armor, weapons, and vanity items, as well as accessories and dyes. Items can be placed on a Mannequin by pressing the ⚷ Open / Activate button on it, which opens a small set of inventory-like slots beneath the player's main inventory. There are three slots for helmet, shirt, and pants, five slots for accessories, and one slot for weapons. In addition, each slot besides the weapons slot has an associated dye slot underneath it. Placing items in these slots immediately displays them on the Mannequin.

name: Greater Healing Potion
description: The Greater Healing Potion is a Hardmode recovery potion that restores 150 health and inflicts Potion Sickness for 60 seconds when used. This duration can be reduced via the Philosopher's Stone or Charm of Myths. Greater Healing Potions can be crafted using materials found in The Hallow or obtained from most Hardmode bosses as well as from Biome Mimics and Martian Saucers.

name: Greater Mana Potion
description: The Greater Mana Potion is a Hardmode recovery potion that restores 200 mana and inflicts the Mana Sickness debuff upon use. Unlike most potions, Greater Mana Potions cannot be crafted, and are instead purchased from the Wizard for 5 SC, or dropped from all Biome Mimics except the Jungle Mimic. It is the third tier of mana potion, succeeding the Lesser Mana Potion and Mana Potion, and preceding the Super Mana Potion.

stats.Debuff: Mana Sickness(Desktop, Console, Old-gen console and Mobile versions)
